  \uses{def:pullback_val_iso, lem:sheafification_comp_pullback_eq_leftadjointuniq}
  Let \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion presented by the presheaf-ring map
  \(\varphi'\), and let \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The unit of the \emph{sheaf}-level
  pullback--pushforward adjunction \(\mathtt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\,f\), transported through
  the forgetful functor \(\mathtt{forget} : \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,Y \to \mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\),
  equals the \emph{presheaf}-level pullback--pushforward unit followed by the sheafification unit and
  the pullback comparison \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\) (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}):
  \[
    \mathtt{forget}\bigl((\mathtt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\,f).\mathrm{unit}.\mathrm{app}\,M\bigr)
      = (\mathtt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\,\varphi').\mathrm{unit}.\mathrm{app}\,M^{\mathrm p}
        \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi').\mathrm{map}\bigl(
          \eta_{(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi')\,M^{\mathrm p}}
          \mathbin{;} \mathtt{forget}((\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,M).\mathrm{hom})\bigr),
  \]
  where \(\eta\) is the unit of the sheafification adjunction on \(Y\) and \(M^{\mathrm p}\) is the
  underlying presheaf of \(M\). This is the lone sheafification-boundary residual of the Bridge~B1
  crux \(H_1\) (\cref{lem:h1inv_app_eq_pullbackval_restrict}); the other three parts of that crux are
  definitional \(\mathtt{forget}\)/\(\mathtt{pushforward}\) functoriality identities.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:pullback_val_iso, lem:sheafification_comp_pullback_eq_leftadjointuniq,
    lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app}
    \leanok
  As shown in the Lean development the identity is, after computing the opaque sheaf unit via the
  concrete \(\mathtt{PullbackConstruction.adjunction}\) (using Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{pullbackIso}\,\varphi = \mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}(\mathtt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\,\varphi)\,
  (\mathtt{PullbackConstruction.adjunction}\,\varphi)\)), equivalent to the single comparison identity
  \[
    (\mathtt{pullbackIso}\,\varphi).\mathrm{inv}.\mathrm{app}\,M
      = (\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,M).\mathrm{hom}
      = \mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}.\mathrm{inv}.\mathrm{app}\,M^{\mathrm p}
        \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{map}\,
          (\varepsilon_M), \qquad \varepsilon := (\mathtt{sheafAdj}_X).\mathrm{counit},
  \]
  i.e.\ the two \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) comparison isos --- one over the right adjoint
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\), the other (\(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\)) over the
  composite right adjoint \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi \circ \mathtt{forget}\) --- agree on \(M\).
  Transposing along the \emph{whole composite} \(\mathtt{homEquiv}\) is circular (it returns the parent
  goal), and \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq\_trans}\) does not apply (distinct right adjoints).

  The identity is the \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\) analogue of Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{Functor.toSheafify\_pullbackSheafificationCompatibility}\)
  (\(\mathtt{Sites/CoverLifting.lean}\): \(362\)--\(382\)), which proves the literal twin --- a
  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) of two \emph{composite} adjunctions, pushed through the forgetful functor
  \(\mathtt{sheafToPresheaf}\) and matched against the sheafification unit \(\mathtt{toSheafify}\). Its
  proof skeleton ports directly:
  % NOTE: (iter-053, review) The lemma is now CLOSED in Lean, but the REALIZED proof does NOT follow
  % the steps 1--4 below. It uses forget-faithfulness (fullyFaithfulForget.map_injective) + an inner
  % presheaf-pullback transpose telescope + the INVERSE-leftAdjointUniq triangle (hAcancel via
  % leftAdjointUniq_inv_app + unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app) + sheafificationCompPullback_eq_leftAdjointUniq.
  % It never invokes Adjunction.comp_unit_app (step 3) and leaves hpin dead. The intro paragraph and the

  % rewrite this enumerate to the realized route. See task_results/lean-vs-blueprint-checker-iter053.md.
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item Transpose along the \emph{innermost} adjunction
      \(\mathtt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\,\varphi'\) (the presheaf-level one whose
      \(\mathtt{homEquiv}\) the crux \(H_1\) already uses), via
      \(\bigl((\mathtt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\,\varphi').\mathtt{homEquiv}\,\_\,\_\bigr).\mathtt{injective}\)
      --- supplying the two objects explicitly --- \emph{not} the composite. This is the step that
      breaks circularity.
    \item Feed the defining unit triangle of the composite comparison,
      \(\mathtt{hpin} := \mathtt{unit\_leftAdjointUniq\_hom\_app}\,A\,B\,M^{\mathrm p}\)
      (\cref{lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app}), where
      \(A = \mathtt{sheafAdj}_X \mathbin{.}\!\mathtt{comp}\,\mathtt{pullbackPPAdj}_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) and
      \(B = \mathtt{pullbackPPAdj}_{\mathrm{pre}} \mathbin{.}\!\mathtt{comp}\,\mathtt{sheafAdj}_Y\)
      (the two composite adjunctions whose left-adjoint comparison \emph{is}
      \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\), \cref{lem:sheafification_comp_pullback_eq_leftadjointuniq}).
    \item Expand both composite units with \(\mathtt{Adjunction.comp\_unit\_app}\) (applied twice),
      exposing the sheafification unit \(\eta\) as an explicit separate factor --- the factor that is
      invisible from the composite \(\mathtt{homEquiv}\) and the reason the naive route is circular.
    \item Transpose back (\(\mathtt{homEquiv\_unit}\)/\(\mathtt{homEquiv\_counit}\) together with unit

\begin{lemma}

  sides are built from the \emph{same} presheaf-level comparison through the \emph{same} transport
  prefix. By construction the tensor-restriction comparison
  \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\,f\) (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}) factors as the shared
  prefix \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,f\)
  --- reducing the restriction to the abstract pullback and moving it inside sheafification ---
  followed by a tail assembled from the structure-sheaf reconciliation; the base-change tensor map
  \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\,f\) (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map}) is built from the identical
  prefix followed by the sheafified oplax tensorator

  left-adjoint--transported strong tensorator

  \(\delta\)-conjugation identity \(\mathtt{pushforward\_mu\_appIso\_collapse}\)

  \(\delta\bigl(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi'\bigr)\) --- the very abstract conjugation

  modules carries a symmetric monoidal structure, and no monoidal structure on the sheaf
  categories \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\) is invoked.

\begin{lemma}



\leanok
[S2: tensor-restriction comparison commutes with further restriction]
  \label{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_restrict_compat}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_restrict_iso_restrict_compat}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso, lem:image_preimage_of_le}
  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
  \texttt{lemma-tensor-product-pullback}: pullback commutes with tensor product
  \emph{functorially}, the functoriality on which the naturality square below rests.}
  Let \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be arbitrary \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and let
  \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart morphism above. Write
  \(T(f) := \mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\,f : (M \otimes_X N)|_f \cong M|_f \otimes N|_f\)
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}). The tensor-restriction comparison is \emph{natural under
  further restriction along \(j\)}: the square
  \[
    \begin{array}{ccc}
      \bigl((M \otimes_X N)|_{\iota_U}\bigr)\big|_j
        & \xrightarrow{\;(\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,T(\iota_U)\;}
        & \bigl(M|_{\iota_U} \otimes_U N|_{\iota_U}\bigr)\big|_j \\[2pt]
      \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle \varrho}
        & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle \varrho \otimes \varrho} \\[2pt]
      (M \otimes_X N)|_{\iota_V}
        & \xrightarrow{\;T(\iota_V)\;}
        & M|_{\iota_V} \otimes_V N|_{\iota_V}
    \end{array}
  \]
  commutes, where \(\varrho\) is the equality-of-opens reindexing of
  \cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}. Equivalently, modulo \(\varrho\),
  \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,T(\iota_U) = T(\iota_V)\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_eq_pullback_tensor_map, lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange, lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural, def:restrictcompreindex, lem:image_preimage_of_le}
  \leanok
  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Modules on Ringed Spaces, Lemma
  % \ref{lemma-tensor-product-pullback} (pullback of $\mathcal{O}$-modules commutes
  % with tensor product, functorially in the two arguments)
  % (read from references/stacks-modules.tex, L2392-2400).
  % SOURCE QUOTE:
  % "\begin{lemma}
  % \label{lemma-tensor-product-pullback}
  % Let $f : (X, \mathcal{O}_X) \to (Y, \mathcal{O}_Y)$ be
  % a morphism of ringed spaces. Let $\mathcal{F}$, $\mathcal{G}$
  % be $\mathcal{O}_Y$-modules. Then
  % $f^*(\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_Y} \mathcal{G})
  % = f^*\mathcal{F} \otimes_{\mathcal{O}_X} f^*\mathcal{G}$

  % "\begin{proof}
  % Omitted.
  % \end{proof}"
  This square is \emph{not} a bespoke leg-by-leg chase: it is base-change bookkeeping built on
  Bridge B1 (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_eq_pullback_tensor_map}). Substitute B1 to rewrite
  both \(T(\iota_U) = \mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_U\) and
  \(T(\iota_V) = \mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_V\) as
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\)-conjugates of the base-change tensor maps
  \(\mathtt{asIso}(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\,\iota_U)\) and
  \(\mathtt{asIso}(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\,\iota_V)\). After this substitution the comparison
  factors \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\) cancel against the reindexing \(\varrho\) by the
  reindex coherence (Bridge B2, used here only through
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\)'s pseudonaturality), and the displayed square reduces to
  the corresponding equation between the \emph{pullback}-world tensor maps. That equation is the
  already-proven composition law \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap\_restrict}\)
  (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange}): for the factorisation
  \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\) it identifies
  \((\mathtt{pullback}\,j)\bigl(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\,\iota_U\bigr)\) with
  \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\,\iota_V\), conjugated by the pullback pseudofunctoriality

  the two module arguments (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural}) carries the per-leg comparisons

  \(\varrho = \mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\) (\cref{def:restrictcompreindex}) threads the
  equality of opens \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\) (\cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}) on both

  \]
  defined as the Step-4 residual of \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\): the composite
  \[
    \theta_M \;=\; \bigl(H1_{\mathtt{dual}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}\bigr)^{-1}
      \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{isoMk}\bigl(V \mapsto \mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\,f\,M\,V\bigr),
  \]
  where \(H1\) is the adjunction-uniqueness isomorphism re-presenting \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) as
  the pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) (Stage~H1 of \cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}), and the
  second factor assembles the per-slice dual transport \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\)
  (\cref{lem:slice_dual_transport}) sectionwise over the thin poset \(\mathrm{Opens}\,Y\); the
  \(\mathtt{isoMk}\) naturality square is the poset-thin (subsingleton) coherence of that family.
  Packaging Step~4 as a named isomorphism in the \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\)-form is what its
  downstream consumers (\cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_eval_apply},
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural}) require: the parent
  \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,f\,M\) (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}) is refactored to consume it as
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}
  \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}\,\theta_M\) (Steps 1--3 wrapping this residual).
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Cone B / L1: the dual base-change comparison is sectionwise an internal-hom evaluation]
  \label{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_eval_apply}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafDual_pullback_comparison_eval_apply}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:internal_hom_eval, def:presheaf_dual}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  This is an alternative eval-based decomposition retained for completeness; it is \emph{not} on the
  critical path, as the immersion-naturality crux \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural}
  closes directly by the built-in naturality of \(\theta\).
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be an open immersion with \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\) and
  \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). On each open section the presheaf-level dual base-change
  comparison \(\theta_M\) (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison})
  acts as the internal-hom \emph{evaluation} of \cref{lem:internal_hom_eval}: a pulled-back dual
  section \(\varphi\) --- an \(\mathcal{O}\)-linear functional on the restricted module --- is carried
  by \(\theta_M\) to the functional on the pulled-back module whose value at a section \(s\) is
  \(\varphi\) evaluated at \(s\), reindexed across the immersion's direct-image
  \(j.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\). In short, the \(\mathtt{app}\) of \(\theta_M\) is, sectionwise, an
  evaluation map of the internal hom (the immersion analogue of the eval-at-\(1\) presentation of
  \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}\) in \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:internal_hom_eval, def:presheaf_dual}
  \leanok
  Unfolding \(\theta_M\) through its construction (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}), the forward
  component at an open \(W \le V\) is the categorical image of the dual-section component of
  \(\varphi\) at the \(j\)-image of \(W\), i.e.\ the evaluation map of the internal hom of
  \cref{lem:internal_hom_eval} reindexed across \(j.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\). This is a direct
  read-off of the definition, sectionwise; no naturality is required for this step.
\end{proof}

\begin{definition}

\leanok
[Cone B / morphism-level dual precomposition operator \(\mathtt{dualPrecompHom}\)]
  \label{def:presheaf_dual_precomp_hom}
  \lean{PresheafOfModules.dualPrecompHom}
  \uses{def:presheaf_dual, def:presheaf_dual_precomp_equiv, def:presheaf_dual_iso_of_iso}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings and \(g : M \to M'\) a morphism of presheaves of
  \(R\)-modules --- \emph{not} required to be invertible. The presheaf dual carries \(g\) to a
  morphism of duals in the reverse direction,
  \[
    \mathtt{dualPrecompHom}\,g \;\colon\; M'^\vee \;\longrightarrow\; M^\vee,
  \]
  defined sectionwise on a fixed open \(U\) as \emph{precomposition} of a dual section by \(g\):
  \[
    (\mathtt{dualPrecompHom}\,g)\,(\varphi) \;=\;
      \bigl(\mathtt{pushforward}_0(\mathtt{Over.forget}\,U)\bigr).\mathrm{map}\,g
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \varphi,
      \qquad \varphi \in \bigl(M'|_U \to R|_U\bigr).
  \]
  This is the \emph{forward leg} of the precomposition equivalence \(\mathtt{dualPrecompEquiv}\)
  (\cref{def:presheaf_dual_precomp_equiv}) stripped of any appeal to an inverse: where
  \(\mathtt{dualPrecompEquiv}\,e\) needs \(e^{-1}\) to package the two one-sided maps into an
  \(R(U)\)-linear equivalence, \(\mathtt{dualPrecompHom}\,g\) keeps only the \(g\)-precomposition map
  and is therefore defined for an arbitrary, possibly non-invertible, \(g\). When
  \(g = e.\mathrm{hom}\) for an isomorphism \(e\), it agrees with the \(\mathrm{hom}\) leg of
  \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e\) (\cref{def:presheaf_dual_iso_of_iso}). It is the contravariant
  functoriality of the presheaf dual \(M \mapsto M^\vee\) (\cref{def:presheaf_dual}) in arbitrary
  morphisms --- the operator needed to conjugate the base-change comparison \(\theta\) by the
  non-invertible restriction map.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Cone B / L2: morphism-level dual transport is precomposition by the restriction map]
  \label{lem:dual_precomp_hom_restrict_apply}
  \lean{PresheafOfModules.dualPrecompHom_restrict_apply}
  \uses{def:presheaf_dual_precomp_hom, def:presheaf_dual}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  With \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) an open immersion as above, the morphism-level dual transport
  \(\mathtt{dualPrecompHom}\,(\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\) (\cref{def:presheaf_dual_precomp_hom}) is, on
  each section, \emph{precomposition} of a dual section (functional) by the underlying section map of
  the restriction morphism \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\): for a dual section \(\varphi\),
  \[
    \bigl(\mathtt{dualPrecompHom}\,(\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\bigr)(\varphi)
      \;=\; (\mathtt{restrict}\,j) \mathbin{;} \varphi
  \]
  sectionwise. The restriction morphism is \emph{not} invertible, and
  \(\mathtt{dualPrecompHom}\) (\cref{def:presheaf_dual_precomp_hom}) is precisely the precomposition
  forward leg that applies in this non-invertible regime --- no inverse of \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\)
  is used. This is the immersion analogue of the precomposition read-off of the dual transport that,
  for a unit automorphism, underlies \cref{lem:presheaf_dualisoofiso_trans}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:presheaf_dual_precomp_hom, def:presheaf_dual}
  \leanok
  Immediate from the definition of \(\mathtt{dualPrecompHom}\)
  (\cref{def:presheaf_dual_precomp_hom}): its sectionwise value at \(\varphi\) is
  \(\bigl(\mathtt{pushforward}_0(\mathtt{Over.forget}\,U)\bigr).\mathrm{map}\,(\mathtt{restrict}\,j)
  \mathbin{;} \varphi\), i.e.\ precomposition of \(\varphi\) by the underlying section map of
  \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\). No invertibility is invoked.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Cone B / L3a: internal-hom evaluation commutes with the immersion restriction map (genuine base-change atom)]
  \label{lem:presheafdual_eval_restrict_commute_apply}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafDual_eval_restrict_commute_apply}
  \uses{lem:internal_hom_eval, lem:image_preimage_of_le}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  This is an alternative eval-based decomposition retained for completeness; it is \emph{not} on the
  critical path, as the immersion-naturality crux \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural}
  closes directly by the built-in naturality of \(\theta\).
  Fix a dual section \(\varphi\) and a section \(s\). Internal-hom evaluation
  (\cref{lem:internal_hom_eval}) commutes with the immersion's restriction map: evaluating then
  restricting equals restricting both arguments then evaluating,
  \[
    \varphi(s)\big|_V \;=\; \bigl(\varphi\big|_V\bigr)\bigl(s\big|_V\bigr),
  \]
  now against the \emph{non-invertible} restriction map \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\) in place of the
  multiplication by a unit scalar that governs the unit-automorphism case
  (\cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}). This is the genuine internal-hom base-change content ---
  the dual-flank analogue of Bridge~B1 (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_eq_pullback_tensor_map}) ---
  and is the immersion analogue of the evaluate/restrict commutation core of
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}. The
  down-set fact \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\) (\cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}) pins the open
  along which both sides are restricted.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:internal_hom_eval, lem:image_preimage_of_le}
  \leanok
  This is exactly the evaluate/restrict-commutation identity already established in the proof of
  \cref{lem:internal_hom_eval}: the restriction \(\varphi|_V\) of a functional inside the dual is its
  restriction to the open \(V\), restriction is functorial, and evaluating the restricted functional
  on the restricted section agrees with restricting the value of the evaluation
  (\(\varphi(s)|_V = (\varphi|_V)(s|_V)\)). Because the restriction map is not invertible on sections,
  this is a genuine naturality of evaluation against \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\), not a scalar
  commutation; the open \(V\) is pinned by \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\)
  (\cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Cone B / L3b: pointwise naturality square of the dual base-change comparison (sectionwise form)]
  \label{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural_apply}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafDual_pullback_restrict_natural_apply}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:image_preimage_of_le}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  With \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) as above, the pointwise naturality square of the dual base-change
  comparison holds: for a section and an element, conjugating \(\theta\) by the morphism-level
  transport \(\mathtt{dualPrecompHom}\,(\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\) on the source equals transporting along
  \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\) on the target. Evaluating both composites at a dual section \(\varphi\)
  and a section \(s\), the two sides agree because the underlying presheaf-of-modules morphism
  \(\theta_M\) (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}) commutes with the immersion's restriction
  map by construction. This is the immersion analogue of the eval-core of
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}; the down-set factorisation
  \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\) (\cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}) pins the open along which both
  sides are restricted.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:image_preimage_of_le}
  \leanok
  The underlying presheaf-of-modules morphism \(\theta_M\)
  (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}) commutes with the immersion's restriction map by
  construction --- this is the built-in naturality of a morphism of presheaves of modules, read off
  here at the immersion \(j\) and the dual section \(\varphi\). Evaluating that naturality identity
  against the section \(s\) --- functoriality of evaluation in its functional argument --- yields the
  pointwise square directly, with no intervening internal-hom evaluation or precomposition
  decomposition. The open \(V\) along which both sides are restricted is pinned by
  \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\) (\cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Immersion-naturality of the presheaf dual base-change comparison (Cone B)]
  \label{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafDual_pullback_restrict_natural}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, def:presheaf_dual_precomp_hom, lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural_apply}
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be an open immersion with \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\), and let
  \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The presheaf-level dual base-change comparison
  \[
    \theta_M : (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathtt{dual}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})
      \;\cong\; \mathtt{dual}\bigl((\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\bigr)
  \]
  --- the internal-hom comparison
  \(f^{*}\mathcal{H}om(M, \mathcal{O}) \to \mathcal{H}om(f^{*}M, f^{*}\mathcal{O})\) packaged as
  \(\theta_M\) (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}) --- is natural in the immersion
  \(j\): conjugating \(\theta\) by the morphism-level transport \(\mathtt{dualPrecompHom}\)
  (\cref{def:presheaf_dual_precomp_hom}) along \(j\) on the source matches its transport along
  \(j\) on the target. This is the immersion analogue of \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality},
  with the unit automorphism there replaced by the restriction map \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural_apply}
  \leanok
  By element extensionality on dual sections. Testing the asserted equality of morphisms against an
  arbitrary dual section \(\varphi\) reduces the immersion-naturality square immediately to the
  \emph{built-in} naturality of the underlying presheaf-of-modules morphism \(\theta_M\)
  (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}): a morphism of presheaves of modules commutes with all
  restriction maps by construction, so its naturality at the immersion \(j\), read off at \(\varphi\),
  is exactly the required square. No \(\mathrm{Iso.ext}\)/sectionwise-\(\mathtt{hom\_ext}\) reduction
  ladder and no eval/transport decomposition is needed; the pointwise statement
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural_apply} packages this naturality of \(\theta_M\)
  directly.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}


\leanok
[Bridge dual-B1: \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\) is the conjugated dual base-change comparison]
  \label{lem:dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison}
  \uses{lem:dual_restrict_iso, def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion and \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), with
  \(\varphi = f.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\). The dual-restriction comparison
  \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,f\,M\) (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}) factors as the shared transport
  prefix followed by the sheafified Step-4 residual \(\theta\):
  \[
    \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,f\,M
      = \bigl(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f\bigr).\mathrm{app}\,(\mathtt{dual}\,M)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \bigl(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,\varphi\bigr).\mathrm{app}\,
          \bigl(\mathtt{dual}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{sheafification}.\mathrm{mapIso}\,(\theta_M),
  \]
  where \(\theta_M = \mathtt{presheafDualPullbackComparison}\,f\,M\)
  (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}) is the presheaf dual base-change comparison. This is
  the dual analogue of Bridge B1 (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_eq_pullback_tensor_map}): both sides
  are the \emph{same} three-factor composite by construction, so the identity is definitional.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:dual_restrict_iso, def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}
  \leanok
  Definitional. The parent \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,f\,M\) (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}) was
  refactored to \emph{consume} the Step-4 comparison \(\theta_M\)
  (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}) as a named residual: by construction it is the composite
  of the restriction-to-pullback transport \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f\), the
  sheafification--pullback reconciliation \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,\varphi\) at the
  presheaf dual \(\mathtt{dual}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\), and the sheafification of \(\theta_M\). The
  displayed equation therefore holds by reflexivity.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Module-naturality of the presheaf dual base-change comparison \(\theta\) (Cone B)]
  \label{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_natural_module}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafDual_pullback_comparison_natural}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, def:presheaf_dual_iso_of_iso, def:presheaf_dual_precomp_hom, lem:slice_dual_transport_app_apply}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion and \(\varphi = f.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\).
  The presheaf dual base-change comparison \(\theta_M\)
  (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}) is natural in the \emph{module} argument \(M\):
  for a morphism \(\phi : M \to M'\) of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), the contravariance of the dual
  produces commuting transport, namely
  \[
    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{dualPrecompHom}\,\phi_{\mathrm{val}}\bigr)
      \mathbin{;} \theta_M
    \;=\; \theta_{M'} \mathbin{;}
      \mathtt{dualPrecompHom}\bigl((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{map}\,\phi_{\mathrm{val}}\bigr),
  \]
  where \(\mathtt{dualPrecompHom}\) (\cref{def:presheaf_dual_precomp_hom}) is the contravariant
  morphism-level dual transport (so that, on an isomorphism \(\phi = e\), it specialises to the
  \(\mathrm{hom}\) leg of \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e\), \cref{def:presheaf_dual_iso_of_iso}). This is
  the module-variant naturality of \(\theta\), distinct from the immersion-variant
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural} (natural in \(j\)).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, def:presheaf_dual_precomp_hom, lem:slice_dual_transport_app_apply}
  \leanok
  The comparison factors (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}) as
  \(\theta_M = (H1_{\mathtt{dual}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}})^{-1} \mathbin{;}
    \mathtt{isoMk}(V \mapsto \mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\,f\,M\,V)\), a composite of two pieces each
  natural in \(M\). The first factor \(H1 = \mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) re-presents
  \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) as the pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\); being a natural
  isomorphism of functors, its component at \(\mathtt{dual}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\) is natural in
  \(\mathtt{dual}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\), hence --- by contravariance of the dual --- natural in \(M\)
  through \(\mathtt{dualPrecompHom}\). The second factor is the \(\mathtt{isoMk}\) of the per-slice
  dual transport \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\,f\,M\), whose sectionwise component
  (\cref{lem:slice_dual_transport_app_apply}) is natural in \(M\): on each slice it reindexes the
  dual-section component across the immersion's direct image and conjugates by the codomain unit
  ring-swap, both of which commute with the precomposition transport
  \(\mathtt{dualPrecompHom}\,\phi_{\mathrm{val}}\). Composing the two naturality squares --- the
  \(H1\) square and the slice-transport square --- yields the displayed module-naturality square for
  \(\theta\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Module-naturality of \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\) (contravariant analogue of S2's tensor naturality)]
  \label{lem:dual_restrict_iso_natural}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.dual_restrict_iso_natural}
  \uses{lem:dual_restrict_iso, lem:dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison, lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_natural_module, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be an open immersion and \(e : M \cong M'\) an isomorphism in
  \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,U\). The dual-restriction comparison \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\)
  (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso}) is natural in the module isomorphism: the \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\)-image
  of \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) equals
  \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\) of the \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\)-image of \(e\), conjugated by the
  \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\) comparisons on both flanks (the dual being contravariant, the
  flanks are taken at \(M'\) and \(M\) respectively),
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,j).\mathrm{mapIso}\,(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e)
      = \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,M'
        \,\ggg\, \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\bigl((\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,j).\mathrm{mapIso}\,e\bigr)
        \,\ggg\, (\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,M)^{-1}.
  \]
  This is the contravariant analogue of the covariant tensor naturality
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_natural}; it is the missing ingredient the Seam-1 split
  (\cref{lem:trivialisation_restrict_em_split}) wraps around the dual constituent, letting
  \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\) commute past the \((\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e^M)^{-1}\) factor.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison, lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_natural_module, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}
  \leanok
  By the dual-B1 factorisation (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison}),
  \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\) is the shared transport prefix
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,j \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) followed by
  the sheafified Step-4 residual \(\theta\). The prefix --- the restriction-to-pullback comparison and
  the sheafification--pullback reconciliation --- is a natural transformation, so it commutes with the
  dual transport of \(e\) by built-in naturality. The residual leg is the sheafification of the
  presheaf comparison \(\theta\), whose module-naturality is
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_natural_module}; sheafification is a functor, so it carries
  that presheaf naturality square to the sheaf level. Composing the prefix and residual naturality
  squares, and rewriting the morphism-level dual transports as the \(\mathrm{hom}\) legs of
  \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\) (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) on the isomorphism \(e\), yields
  the displayed conjugated identity.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Module-naturality of \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\) (S2's bifunctorial naturality)]
  \label{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_natural}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_restrict_iso_natural}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso, lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_eq_pullback_tensor_map, lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural, def:restrictfunctorisopullback, def:tensorobjisoofiso}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion and \(a : M \cong M'\), \(b : N \cong N'\)
  isomorphisms in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The tensor-restriction comparison
  \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\,f\) (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}) is natural in the two
  module isomorphisms: the \(\mathtt{restrict}\,f\)-image of \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,a\,b\)
  (\cref{def:tensorobjisoofiso}) equals \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\) of the \(\mathtt{restrict}\,f\)-images
  of \(a\) and \(b\), conjugated by the \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\,f\) comparisons,
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,f).\mathrm{mapIso}\,(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,a\,b)
      = \mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\,f\,M\,N
        \,\ggg\, \mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\bigl((\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,f).\mathrm{mapIso}\,a\bigr)
          \bigl((\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,f).\mathrm{mapIso}\,b\bigr)
        \,\ggg\, (\mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\,f\,M'\,N')^{-1}.
  \]
  This is the covariant ingredient the Seam-1 split (\cref{lem:trivialisation_restrict_em_split})
  wraps around the trivialisation step, letting \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\) commute past the
  \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,e^M\,(\cdots)\) of constituent~(2).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_eq_pullback_tensor_map, lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural, def:restrictfunctorisopullback}
  \leanok
  The naturality square is established at the \(\mathrm{hom}\) level and the iso identity then follows
  by cancelling the comparison at \((M', N')\). Promote both tensor-restriction comparisons to the
  pullback world via Bridge B1 (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_eq_pullback_tensor_map}), reducing the
  square to the naturality of the pullback--tensor comparison \(\delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\)
  (\cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural}) sandwiched between two naturality squares of the
  restriction--pullback comparison \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f\)
  (\cref{def:restrictfunctorisopullback}) --- one for \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}.\mathrm{hom}\)
  on the leading leg and one for its inverse on each per-factor tail. The shared prefix
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}.\mathrm{app} \mathbin{;} \delta_{\mathrm{sheaf}}\) cancels and the
  two per-leg tails match by inverse-naturality of \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\), closing the
  square.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}


\leanok
[Bridge dual-(b): composition law of \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) over an immersion factorisation]
  \label{lem:sheafificationcomppullback_restrict}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.sheafificationCompPullback_restrict}
  % NOTE (iter-080 review, lvb major #1): CLOSED iter-080 by the one-liner
  % `exact sheafificationCompPullback_comp j U.ι P` (Sq1 specialised). The realized proof's SOLE
  % dependency is lem:sheafificationcomppullback_comp; the other three \uses
  % (def:restrictcompreindex, lem:toringcatsheafhom_comp_hom_reconcile, lem:image_preimage_of_le)
  % are over-credited (spurious DAG edges). blueprint-writer: trim \uses to the single real dep.
  \uses{lem:sheafificationcomppullback_comp, def:restrictcompreindex, lem:toringcatsheafhom_comp_hom_reconcile, lem:image_preimage_of_le}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart morphism above, with \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\),
  and let \(P\) be a presheaf of modules over \(X\). Write
  \(\mathtt{SCP}\,g = \mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,(\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\,g)\) for the
  sheafification--pullback comparison, \(a_X\) for sheafification on \(X\), and
  \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,j\,\iota_U\) for the pullback pseudofunctoriality iso
  \(\iota_V^{*} \cong j^{*}\!\circ\iota_U^{*}\). Then the comparison for the composite immersion
  \(\iota_V\) factors through those for \(\iota_U\) and for \(j\), conjugated by
  \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,j\,\iota_U\):
  \[
    (\mathtt{SCP}\,\iota_V).\mathrm{app}\,P
      = \bigl((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{app}\,(a_X P)\bigr)^{-1}
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\bigl((\mathtt{SCP}\,\iota_U).\mathrm{app}\,P\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{SCP}\,j).\mathrm{app}\bigl((\mathtt{pullback}\,\iota_U)\,P\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; a_V\bigl((\mathtt{pullbackComp}_{\mathrm{pre}}\,\iota_U\,j).\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,P\bigr),
  \]
  where \(a_V\) is sheafification on \(V\) and \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}_{\mathrm{pre}}\,\iota_U\,j\) is the
  \emph{presheaf}-level pullback pseudofunctoriality isomorphism \(\iota_V^{*} \cong \iota_U^{*}\!\circ j^{*}\)
  on presheaves of modules. Its sheafification supplies a fourth, dual-independent leg: this leg, not a
  \(\mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\) factor, is what threads the equality of opens
  \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\) through the comparison.
  The statement is \emph{independent of the dual}: \(P\) is an arbitrary presheaf (here instantiated at
  \(P = \mathtt{dual}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\)).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:sheafificationcomppullback_comp, def:restrictcompreindex, lem:toringcatsheafhom_comp_hom_reconcile, lem:image_preimage_of_le}
  \leanok
  This is the immersion-factorisation specialisation of the generic composition coherence Sq1
  (\cref{lem:sheafificationcomppullback_comp}), which for composable \(h, f\) and \emph{any} presheaf
  already identifies \((\mathtt{SCP}\,(h\circ f)).\mathrm{app}\) with the
  \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)-conjugate of the \(f\)- and \(h\)-instances. Apply Sq1 with \(h = j\),
  \(f = \iota_U\) and the presheaf \(P\). The composite ring map of \(\iota_V = j \mathbin{;} \iota_U\)
  is reconciled with the whiskered composite of the \(\iota_U\)- and \(j\)-maps by
  \(\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom\_comp}\) (\cref{lem:toringcatsheafhom_comp_hom_reconcile}), so Sq1
  specialises to the displayed equation. Finally the reindexing
  \(\varrho = \mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\) (\cref{def:restrictcompreindex}) threads the equality of
  opens \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\) (\cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}) on the restriction flank,
  matching the \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\)-image presentation used at the seam. No dual-specific input
  enters: this is the \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\)-portion of the tensor base-change law
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange}, the analogue of the
  \(\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\)-composition \cref{lem:pullback_val_iso_comp}.
\end{proof}

\begin{definition}

\leanok
[Cone B / c.1: object-identification iso \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}} \cong (M|_f)_{\mathrm{val}}\)]
  \label{def:pushforward_obj_val_restrict_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforwardObjValRestrictIso}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, def:restrictfunctorisopullback, lem:sheafification_comp_pullback_eq_leftadjointuniq}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion and \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), and let
  \(\beta\) be the whiskered direct-image natural isomorphism whose pushforward is
  left-adjoint-equivalent to \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) --- the data fixed inside
  \cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}. There is a canonical isomorphism of presheaves of modules
  over \(Y\)
  \[
    (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}
      \;\cong\; \bigl(M|_f\bigr)_{\mathrm{val}},
  \]
  identifying the direct image of the underlying presheaf \(M_{\mathrm{val}}\) with the underlying
  presheaf of the restricted module \(M|_f = \mathtt{restrict}\,f\,M\). It is the foundational
  object-level identification through which the pushforward presentation of \(\theta\) is reconciled with
  the restriction-world form consumed downstream.
\end{definition}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, def:restrictfunctorisopullback, lem:sheafification_comp_pullback_eq_leftadjointuniq}
  \leanok
  Compose two canonical identifications. First, the adjunction-uniqueness isomorphism built inside
  \cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison} re-presents the abstract presheaf pullback
  \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) as the pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) on objects, giving
  \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}} \cong
  (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\). Second, the \(\mathrm{val}\)-component of
  the restriction--pullback comparison \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,f\)
  (\cref{def:restrictfunctorisopullback}) followed by the sheafify-then-pullback identification
  (\cref{lem:sheafification_comp_pullback_eq_leftadjointuniq}) relates
  \((\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\) to \((M|_f)_{\mathrm{val}}\). Both
  ingredients are canonical isomorphisms already in hand; their composite is the asserted iso, and
  poset-thinness of \(\mathrm{Opens}\,Y\) makes the naturality square of the assembled family automatic.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[The presheaf pullback pseudofunctoriality isomorphism \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)]
  \label{lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp}
  \lean{PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  For composable ring-presheaf maps \(\varphi, \psi\), the composition of the two presheaf pullback
  functors is canonically isomorphic to the pullback along the composite:
  \[
    \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi \,\circ\, \mathtt{pullback}\,\psi
      \;\cong\; \mathtt{pullback}\,(\varphi \mathbin{;} \psi).
  \]
  By construction it \emph{is} the left-adjoint composition isomorphism
  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) of the three pullback--pushforward adjunctions, reconciled by the
  pushforward composition \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\); this definitional identity is the content of
  \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_pullbackcomp_inv}. It is leg~1 of the cocycle
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Restriction along a composite as a composition of restrictions \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\)]
  \label{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorComp}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  For composable open immersions \(h : Z \hookrightarrow Y\) and \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\), restriction
  of sheaves of modules along the composite is canonically isomorphic to the composition of the two
  restrictions:
  \[
    \mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,(h \mathbin{;} f)
      \;\cong\; \mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,f \,\circ\, \mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,h .
  \]
  Its contravariant image under the dual --- \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\)
  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) of the forgetful image of
  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,h\,f).\mathrm{app}\,M\) --- is the dual-target pseudofunctoriality
  leg~4 of the cocycle \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Mate of a \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) comparison is the identity]
  \label{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointuniq_hom}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Adjunction.conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointUniq_hom}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(F, F' : C_0 \to C_1\) be two left adjoints of one and the same right adjoint
  \(G : C_1 \to C_0\), via \(\mathrm{adj}_1 : F \dashv G\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_2 : F' \dashv G\). Then the
  mate (conjugate) of the comparison isomorphism \((\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\,\mathrm{adj}_1\,\mathrm{adj}_2).\mathrm{hom}\)
  under the conjugation bijection \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv}\,\mathrm{adj}_2\,\mathrm{adj}_1\) is the identity
  natural transformation \(\mathbf{1}_G\). This is the abstract content behind every
  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) cocycle: the comparison transports the unit of one adjunction to the
  other and is therefore mate to \(\mathbf{1}_G\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}


\leanok
  Unfold \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) as the symmetrisation of the conjugate-iso bijection and apply the
  Mathlib computation \(\mathtt{conjugateIsoEquiv\_symm\_apply\_inv}\) followed by the
  \(\mathtt{Equiv.apply\_symm\_apply}\) round-trip, which collapses the mate to \(\mathbf{1}_G\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Mate of the \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) hom is the reconciling inverse]
  \label{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Adjunction.conjugateEquiv_leftAdjointCompIso_hom}
  \uses{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointuniq_hom}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Given three adjunctions \(\mathrm{adj}_{01} : F_{01} \dashv G_{10}\),
  \(\mathrm{adj}_{12} : F_{12} \dashv G_{21}\), \(\mathrm{adj}_{02} : F_{02} \dashv G_{20}\) and a
  right-adjoint composition isomorphism \(e : G_{21} \circ G_{10} \cong G_{20}\), the mate (conjugate)
  of \((\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\,\mathrm{adj}_{01}\,\mathrm{adj}_{12}\,\mathrm{adj}_{02}\,e).\mathrm{hom}\)
  under \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv}\,\mathrm{adj}_{02}\,(\mathrm{adj}_{01}.\mathrm{comp}\,\mathrm{adj}_{12})\)
  equals \(e.\mathrm{inv}\). This is the companion of Mathlib's
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_leftAdjointCompIso\_inv}\) (which computes the conjugate of the \(\mathrm{inv}\)).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}


\leanok
  By \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_leftAdjointCompIso\_inv}\) the conjugate of the \(\mathrm{inv}\) is
  \(e.\mathrm{hom}\); rewriting \(e.\mathrm{hom}\) as that conjugate and using functoriality of the
  conjugation bijection under composition (\(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_comp}\)) together with
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_id}\) shows the conjugate of the \(\mathrm{hom}\) post-composed with
  \(e.\mathrm{hom}\) is the identity. Cancelling the mono \(e.\mathrm{hom}\) identifies the conjugate
  with \(e.\mathrm{inv}\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Explicit component of the \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) hom]
  \label{lem:leftadjointcompiso_hom_app}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Adjunction.leftAdjointCompIso_hom_app}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Adjunction/CompositionIso.lean}).}
  Given three adjunctions \(\mathrm{adj}_{01} : F_{01} \dashv G_{10}\),
  \(\mathrm{adj}_{12} : F_{12} \dashv G_{21}\), \(\mathrm{adj}_{02} : F_{02} \dashv G_{20}\) and a
  right-adjoint composition isomorphism \(e : G_{21} \circ G_{10} \cong G_{20}\), the hom of the
  left-adjoint composition isomorphism is \emph{fully explicit} at each object \(X\):
  \[
    (\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\,\mathrm{adj}_{01}\,\mathrm{adj}_{12}\,\mathrm{adj}_{02}\,e).\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,X
      \;=\; F_{12}.\mathrm{map}\bigl(F_{01}.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathrm{adj}_{02}.\mathrm{unit}.\mathrm{app}\,X)\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; F_{12}.\mathrm{map}\bigl(F_{01}.\mathrm{map}\,(e.\mathrm{inv}.\mathrm{app}\,\cdot)\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; F_{12}.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathrm{adj}_{01}.\mathrm{counit}.\mathrm{app}\,\cdot)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathrm{adj}_{12}.\mathrm{counit}.\mathrm{app}\,\cdot,
  \]
  i.e. the unit of \(\mathrm{adj}_{02}\) pushed through \(F_{01}\) and \(F_{12}\), the reconciling
  \(e.\mathrm{inv}\), and the two counits of \(\mathrm{adj}_{01}\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_{12}\). When the
  reconciling iso is the identity (\(e = \mathtt{Iso.refl}\)) the middle \(e.\mathrm{inv}\) factor drops.
  This is the section-level component companion of the mate characterisation
  \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom}.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Abstract \(H1\) cocycle: \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) intertwines two \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\)s]
  \label{lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq_leftAdjointCompIso_comm}
  \uses{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointuniq_hom, lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(F_\bullet\) and \(P_\bullet\) be two families of left adjoints sharing the right adjoints
  \(G_{\bullet\bullet}\) level by level: \(\mathrm{adjF}_{ij} : F_{ij} \dashv G_{ji}\) and
  \(\mathrm{adjP}_{ij} : P_{ij} \dashv G_{ji}\) for \(ij \in \{01, 12, 02\}\), together with a single
  shared right-adjoint composition isomorphism \(e : G_{21} \circ G_{10} \cong G_{20}\). Write
  \(H1_{ij} = \mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\,\mathrm{adjF}_{ij}\,\mathrm{adjP}_{ij} : F_{ij} \cong P_{ij}\)
  for the comparison isomorphisms and \(FC, PC\) for the two
  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\)s built from the \emph{same} \(e\). Then the \(H1\)s intertwine the two
  composition isomorphisms:
  \[
    FC.\mathrm{hom} \;\mathbin{;}\; (H1_{02}).\mathrm{hom}
      \;=\; \bigl((H1_{01}).\mathrm{hom} \,\triangleright\, F_{12}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \bigl(P_{01} \,\triangleleft\, (H1_{12}).\mathrm{hom}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; PC.\mathrm{hom} .
  \]
  This is the abstract \(H1\) cocycle instantiated in the Cone~B/c.2 lemma
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle}, and the generic engine behind
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_restrictFunctorComp\_inv}\)
  (\cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_restrictfunctorcomp_inv}): both reduce a composite comparison to a chain
  over the two composition isomorphisms.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointuniq_hom, lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom}
  \leanok
  Apply the conjugation bijection \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv}\), which is injective, to both sides. On the
  left, functoriality under composition turns the mate of \(FC.\mathrm{hom} \mathbin{;} (H1_{02}).\mathrm{hom}\)
  into the mate of \(H1_{02}\) composed with the mate of \(FC.\mathrm{hom}\); by
  \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointuniq_hom} the first is \(\mathbf{1}\) and by
  \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom} the second is \(e.\mathrm{inv}\), so the left mate is
  \(e.\mathrm{inv}\). On the right, the threefold composite splits under
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_comp}\); the two whiskered \(H1\) factors collapse via
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_whiskerRight}\) and \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_whiskerLeft}\) to identities by
  \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointuniq_hom}, and the \(PC.\mathrm{hom}\) factor becomes
  \(e.\mathrm{inv}\) by \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom}. Both mates equal \(e.\mathrm{inv}\),
  so the two morphisms agree.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Cone B / c.2(a): cocycle law of the adjunction-uniqueness iso \(H1\) over the immersion factorisation]
  \leanok
  \label{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafDualH1Cocycle}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp, lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp, lem:conjugate_equiv_pullbackcomp_inv, lem:adjunction_leftadjointuniq_trans, lem:conjugate_equiv_restrictfunctorcomp_inv, lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(h : Z \hookrightarrow Y\) and \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be composable open immersions, write
  \(\varphi_g = g.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}.\mathrm{hom}\), and let
  \[
    H1_g \;=\; (\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\,\cdots).\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\,
      (\mathtt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\,\varphi_g)
      \;\colon\; \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_g \;\cong\; \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_g
  \]
  be the left-adjoint-uniqueness isomorphism re-presenting the presheaf pullback as the pushforward
  (Stage~H1 of \cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}). Then \(H1\) satisfies a \emph{cocycle}
  (composition) law over the factorisation \(h \mathbin{;} f\): on the pullback (source) flank it is
  threaded by the presheaf pseudofunctoriality iso \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\)
  (\cref{lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp}) and on the pushforward (target) flank by the
  restriction-composition iso \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,h\,f\)
  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}, equivalently the pushforward composition
  \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\)). Writing \(FC = \mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) for the left-adjoint
  composition isomorphism of the three pushforward adjunctions
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_g \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi_g\), reconciled by the presheaf
  pushforward composition \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\), the realized cocycle is
  the \emph{hom}-form identity
  \[
    FC.\mathrm{hom} \;\mathbin{;}\; (H1_{h \mathbin{;} f}).\mathrm{hom}
      \;=\; \bigl((H1_f).\mathrm{hom} \,\triangleright\, \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_h\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \bigl(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_f \,\triangleleft\, (H1_h).\mathrm{hom}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h).\mathrm{hom} ,
  \]
  stated as a faithful \(\forall\)-strengthening over the three pushforward adjunctions
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_g \dashv \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi_g\)
  (\(g \in \{f,\, h,\, h\mathbin{;}f\}\)); the consumer instantiates it with the matching
  \(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj}\) adjunctions. Here \(\triangleright\) is right-whiskering
  (\(\alpha \triangleright F = \mathtt{whiskerRight}\,\alpha\,F\)) and \(\triangleleft\) left-whiskering
  (\(F \triangleleft \alpha = \mathtt{whiskerLeft}\,F\,\alpha\)). Inverting the identity (swap \(f\)
  and \(h\) in the two middle legs and replace \((\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h).\mathrm{hom}\)
  by its \(\mathrm{inv}\)) yields the form in which \(\theta\)'s \(H1\)-prefix is recombined inside
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}.
  This is the dual-flank analogue of \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_pullbackcomp_inv}
  (\(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_pullbackComp\_inv}\)) and of
  \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv\_restrictFunctorComp\_inv}\)
  (\cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_restrictfunctorcomp_inv}). This is the \(H1\)-flank of the cocycle
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp, lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp, lem:conjugate_equiv_pullbackcomp_inv, lem:adjunction_leftadjointuniq_trans, lem:conjugate_equiv_restrictfunctorcomp_inv, lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm}
  \leanok
  Each \(H1_g\) is a \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\) comparison of two left adjoints of the shared right
  adjoint \(\mathtt{pushforward}\). The composite-immersion comparison \(H1_{h \mathbin{;} f}\)
  decomposes by transitivity of \(\mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\)
  (\cref{lem:adjunction_leftadjointuniq_trans}) once the composite pullback
  \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_{h \mathbin{;} f}\) is identified with the iterated pullback
  \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_h \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_f\) through
  \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\) (\cref{lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp}), and the
  composite pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_{h \mathbin{;} f}\) with the iterated pushforward
  through \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,h\,f\) (equivalently \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\),
  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}). Because \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) is \emph{defined} as
  the left-adjoint composition isomorphism \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\), the reconciliation of these
  two pseudofunctor isos on the conjugate is exactly \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_pullbackcomp_inv},
  instantiated at the present adjunctions; substituting it collapses the conjugate to the displayed
  three-factor chain. Concretely, this is a direct instantiation of the abstract \(H1\) cocycle
  \cref{lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm} at the three pushforward/pullback adjunction pairs,
  with \(F_\bullet = \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\), \(P_\bullet = \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) and the
  shared composition iso \(e = \mathtt{pushforwardComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\); the \(P\)-side
  \(\mathtt{leftAdjointCompIso}\) is definitionally \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,\varphi_f\,\varphi_h\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Generic \(H1\)-cancellation skeleton for the c.2 cocycle]
  \label{lem:c2_assemble}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.c2_assemble}
  \uses{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle, lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom, lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Work in an arbitrary category \(C\) with objects \(A_1, \dots, A_9\) and the morphisms appearing in
  the c.2 reduction. Given the eight structural facts of that reduction --- the two iso cancellations
  \(\mathtt{aHinv} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{aH} = \mathbf{1}\) (of \(H1_{h \mathbin{;} f}\)) and
  \(\mathtt{fcinv} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{fc} = \mathbf{1}\) (of \(FC\)), the abstract \(H1\) cocycle
  \(\mathtt{fc} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{aH} = \mathtt{phf} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{hh} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{pc}\)
  (instantiating \cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle}), the \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\) cancellation

  slide \(\mathtt{hh} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{Pfhif} = \mathtt{Pushhif} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{hhdmf}\), the
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_h\)-functoriality fold

  \(\mathtt{hhdmf} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{Hhinv} = \mathbf{1}\), and the residual \((**)\)
  \(\mathtt{fc} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{s} = \mathtt{pushSDTf} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sDTh} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{p3}\)
  --- the c.2 goal identity
  \(\mathtt{aHinv} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{s}
    = \mathtt{p0} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{Pfhif} \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{Hhinv} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sDTh}) \mathbin{;} \mathtt{p3}\)
  holds. This is the instance-agnostic engine that performs the entire \(H1\)-cancellation of
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}; the consumer supplies its eight hypotheses at
  the concrete pushforward/pullback morphisms (the cocycle hypothesis being
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle}, itself \cref{lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm} at the
  three pushforward adjunctions, reconciled by \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom}).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle, lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom, lem:leftadjointuniq_leftadjointcompiso_comm}
  \leanok
  Pure associativity and identity rewriting. Pre-compose the cocycle goal with \(\mathtt{fc}\) and use
  the cocycle hypothesis together with the residual \((**)\); the \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)
  cancellation, the \(H1_h\) naturality slide, the \(H1_h\) round-trip and the functoriality fold then
  collapse the middle in turn, yielding \(\mathtt{fc} \mathbin{;} (\text{RHS}) = \mathtt{fc} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{s}\).
  Cancelling the iso \(\mathtt{fc}\) (via \(\mathtt{fcinv}\)) and then the iso \(\mathtt{aH}\) (via
  \(\mathtt{aHinv}\)) gives the stated identity.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}[Bridge dual-(c): cocycle law of the presheaf dual base-change comparison \(\theta\) over \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\)]
  \leanok
  \label{lem:presheafdual_pullback_comparison_restrict}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafDualPullbackComparison_restrict}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle, lem:slice_dual_transport_app_apply, def:pushforward_obj_val_restrict_iso, lem:presheafofmodules_pullbackcomp, lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso, lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural, lem:image_preimage_of_le, lem:leftadjointcompiso_hom_app, lem:slice_dual_transport_comp, lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom, lem:c2_assemble, lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_unit_app_app_apply, lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_counit_app_app_apply}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart morphism, \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\), and
  \(M \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The presheaf dual base-change comparison \(\theta\)
  (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}) satisfies a \emph{cocycle} (composition) law over the
  immersion factorisation, in the \emph{exact dual shape} of the tensor composition coherence
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange}: the \(\iota_V\)-instance \(\theta_{\iota_V, M}\) factors
  through the \(j\)-pullback of the \(\iota_U\)-instance and the \(j\)-instance \emph{at the pushforward
  object} \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_{\iota_U}).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\) --- the
  base-change image of \(M_{\mathrm{val}}\), \emph{not} the restricted module \(M|_{\iota_U}\) ---
  conjugated by the presheaf pullback pseudofunctoriality iso \(\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,j\,\iota_U\) on the
  source and by its contravariant transport on the dual target:
  \[
    \theta_{\iota_V, M}
      = (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{inv}_{\,\mathtt{dual}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}
        \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{pullback}\,j)\bigl(\theta_{\iota_U, M}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \theta_{j,\ M|_{\iota_U}}
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\bigl(\mathtt{forget}.\mathrm{mapIso}\,
          ((\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{app}\,M)\bigr).\mathrm{hom}.
  \]
  Stating the middle factor at the restricted module \(M|_{\iota_U}\) is type-correct because the
  object-identification iso \(\mathtt{pushforwardObjValRestrictIso}\)
  (\cref{def:pushforward_obj_val_restrict_iso}, bridge c.1) is \(\mathtt{Iso.refl}\): the underlying
  presheaf \((M|_{\iota_U})_{\mathrm{val}}\) is \emph{definitionally} the pushforward object
  \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_{\iota_U}).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\) (the presheaf-level pushforward
  composition being the identity), so the codomain of \((\mathtt{pullback}\,j)(\theta_{\iota_U, M})\)
  --- namely
  \((\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_j).\mathrm{obj}\bigl(\mathtt{dual}((\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi_{\iota_U}).\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})\bigr)\)
  --- already matches the domain of \(\theta_{j,\ M|_{\iota_U}}\) with no intervening transport. This is
  the form consumed by \cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat}. On the dual (target) flank the
  contravariant transport is realised by \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\)
  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}) of the forgetful image of the restriction-composition iso
  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{app}\,M\)
  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}); this pseudofunctoriality leg appears in place of the
  \(\varrho \otimes \varrho\) of the tensor side. This is the dual analogue of the composition role of
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural} inside \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}

  one isolated pushforward-flank identity that touches \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\)'s internals.
  Working sectionwise (each \(\theta.\mathrm{hom}\) is a presheaf morphism), abbreviate
  \(dM = \mathtt{dual}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\),

  \(FC = \mathtt{pushforwardComp}\), and
  \(\varrho = (\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,j\,\iota_U).\mathrm{app}\,M\), and expand each instance via
  \cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison} as
  \(\theta_g = (H1_g)^{-1} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sDT}_g\). The reduction runs in six steps.
  \begin{enumerate}
    \item \textbf{Strip the leading inverse.} Left-multiply both sides by
      \(H1_{\iota_V}.\mathrm{hom}\) (using the isomorphism inverse-composition identity); the left side
      becomes \(\mathtt{sDT}_{\iota_V}\) and the right side acquires the prefix
      \(H1_{\iota_V}.\mathrm{hom}\).
    \item \textbf{Expose the cocycle prefix.} Left-multiply by the \(\mathtt{pushforwardComp}\) counit
      \(FC.\mathrm{hom}_{dM}\), legitimate because it is an isomorphism (hence an epimorphism); the
      left flank now reads \(FC.\mathrm{hom}_{dM} \mathbin{;} H1_{\iota_V}.\mathrm{hom}_{dM}\).
    \item \textbf{Substitute cocycle (a).} By the abstract \(H1\) cocycle
      \cref{lem:presheafdual_h1_cocycle} this prefix equals
      \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_j).\mathrm{map}\,(H1_{\iota_U}.\mathrm{hom})

        \mathbin{;} \mathtt{pullbackComp}.\mathrm{hom}_{dM}\),

        FC.\mathrm{hom}_{dM} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sDT}_{\iota_V}

  The residual \((**)\) is the pushforward-flank pseudofunctoriality of
  \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\) --- the only step that touches the transport's internals --- and it
  plays the dual role of \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_natural} inside the proven tensor analogue

  codomain \((\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_{\iota_V}).\mathrm{obj}\,dM\) are themselves not defeq, because
  the \(\mathtt{opensFunctor}\) of a composite open immersion is not the composite of the

  is \emph{fully explicit} via the Mathlib component formula

  \textbf{The close route.} Expose \(FC.\mathrm{hom}\) through the component formula

  \cref{lem:conjugate_equiv_leftadjointcompiso_hom} is an alternative reading --- it identifies the
  conjugate \(\mathtt{conjugateEquiv}\,FC.\mathrm{hom} = e.\mathrm{inv} = \mathbf 1\), but delivers only

  coherence}. The leading \(FC.\mathrm{hom}\) is handled sectionwise via \cref{lem:leftadjointcompiso_hom_app}: it is the composite of the pushforward adjunction's
  unit and two counits, and \emph{each} of these, evaluated at a section, is --- by the triangle-component
  identities \cref{lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_unit_app_app_apply} and
  \cref{lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_counit_app_app_apply} --- precisely a presheaf restriction map

  iso cancellations of \(H1_{h \mathbin{;} f}\) and \(FC\) at \(dM\), the abstract \(H1\) cocycle~(a)

  \(H1_h.\mathrm{hom}\) naturality, the \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta_h\)-functoriality fold, the
  \(H1_h\) round-trip at \(d(M|_{\iota_U})\), and the residual \((**)\) --- and assembles the c.2

  directly to the c.2 goal, and the definitional equality between the two categories is absorbed into
  the abstract hypothesis statements rather than appearing as an additional verification condition.
\end{proof}

  (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_restrictfunctorcomp}). Assembling the leg-for-leg identities yields the
  displayed composite.
\end{proof}

        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\bigl((\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,hf).\mathrm{app}\,M\bigr)^{-1}.

  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,(\mathrm{rfl}) = \mathrm{Iso.refl}\) (section-level, since its hom is

\begin{definition}
[Oplax-monoidal unit map $\eta$ of the presheaf pullback --- conceptual anchor]
  \label{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_eta}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Functor.OplaxMonoidal.η}
  \mathlibok
  \uses{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  For any oplax monoidal functor \(F\), Mathlib supplies the \emph{unit comparator}
  \(\eta_F : F(\mathbf 1) \to \mathbf 1\), the oplax counterpart of the unit map. Applied to the
  oplax-monoidal presheaf pullback \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\)
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}) along the chart immersion
  \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\), \(\varphi = j.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\), it furnishes the bare
  oplax unit comparison
  \[
    \eta(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi) :
      (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U \longrightarrow \mathbf 1_V .
  \]
  This is the \emph{conceptual} pullback-flank unit map. Its invertibility is \emph{not} asserted
  here. The actual pullback-side comparator \(p\) used in the dual base-change theory is built
  instead as
  the H1 transport of the pushforward comparator \(q\); see
  \cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}.
\end{definition}

\begin{definition}

\leanok
[Pullback-side unit comparator $p$ --- the H1 realization]
  \label{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafPullbackUnitIso}
  \uses{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso, lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app, lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_unit_app_app_apply, lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) be the chart immersion, \(\varphi = j.\mathtt{toRingCatSheafHom}\)
  the pullback ring map, and \(\beta\) the corresponding (sectionwise-bijective) pushforward ring
  map. The pullback-side unit comparator used throughout the dual base-change theory is the
  isomorphism
  \[
    p := \mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,j :
      (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U \;\cong\; \mathbf 1_V ,
  \]
  realized \emph{not} as the bare oplax \(\eta\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_eta}) but through
  the \emph{H1 reconciliation}. Both \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) and the open-immersion
  pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) are left adjoints of the same right adjoint
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\); the left-adjoint-uniqueness isomorphism
  \[
    H_1 := \mathtt{leftAdjointUniq}\bigl(\mathtt{pushforwardPushforwardAdj},\,
      \mathtt{pullbackPushforwardAdjunction}\,\varphi\bigr) :
      \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
  \]
  identifies the two left adjoints (\cref{lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app},
  \cref{lem:pushforwardpushforwardadj_unit_app_app_apply}). Evaluated at the structure-sheaf unit
  \(\mathbf 1_U\), it carries the pullback flank to the pushforward flank, on which the pushforward
  comparator \(q = \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,j\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso})
  contracts to \(\mathbf 1_V\). Composing,
  \[
    p \;=\; (H_1.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_U)^{-1} \,\mathbin{;}\, q ,
  \]
  the H1-transport of \(q\). This is exactly the identification by which the two flank comparators
  are matched at the structure-sheaf unit in the \(\theta\)-at-unit bridge
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural}.
\end{definition}

\begin{definition}
[Lax-monoidal unit map $\varepsilon$ of the presheaf pushforward --- conceptual anchor]
  \label{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_eps}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Functor.LaxMonoidal.ε}
  \mathlibok
  \uses{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  For any lax monoidal functor \(G\), Mathlib supplies the \emph{unit comparator}
  \(\varepsilon_G : \mathbf 1 \to G(\mathbf 1)\). Applied to the lax-monoidal presheaf pushforward
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pushforward}\,\beta\) (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}) along
  the chart immersion, it gives the bare lax unit comparison
  \[
    \varepsilon(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta) :
      \mathbf 1_V \longrightarrow (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U ,
  \]
  the presheaf analogue of \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.unitToPushforwardObjUnit}\). This is the
  \emph{conceptual} pushforward-flank unit map; its invertibility is supplied not by the lax
  structure but by the strong monoidality of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) for an open immersion
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}), and the resulting comparator \(q\) is recorded in
  \cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Strong monoidality of the open-immersion presheaf pushforward $\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta$]
  \label{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafPushforwardBetaMonoidal}
  \uses{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal, lem:restrictscalars_monoidal_of_bijective}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  Let \(f : Y \to X\) be an open immersion and \(\beta = \mathtt{whiskerRight}\,\alpha\,
  (\mathtt{forget}_2)\) the induced structure-ring comparison, where
  \(\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,U = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,U)^{-1}\). Since \(f\) is an open immersion each
  \(\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,U\) is invertible, hence \(\beta\) is sectionwise bijective. Consequently the
  presheaf pushforward factors as the strong-monoidal composite
  \[
    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \;=\;
      \mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}} \,\circ\, \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta ,
  \]
  in which the topological pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}}\) is strong monoidal
  and \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta\) is strong monoidal because \(\beta\) is sectionwise
  bijective (\(\mathtt{restrictScalarsMonoidalOfBijective}\)). Hence \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\)
  carries a \emph{strong}-monoidal structure, upgrading the ambient lax instance
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_laxmonoidal}); in particular its unit comparator
  \(\varepsilon\) and tensorator \(\mu\) are isomorphisms.
\end{lemma}

\begin{definition}

\leanok
[Pushforward-side unit comparator $q$ --- the strong-monoidal $\varepsilon$ inverse]
  \label{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafPushforwardUnitIso}
  \uses{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  In the open-immersion setting above, the strong monoidality of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\)
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}) makes the unit comparator an isomorphism
  \(\varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta) :
    \mathbf 1_V \cong (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U\). The pushforward-side
  unit comparator is its inverse,
  \[
    q := \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)^{-1} :
      (\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta).\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1_U \;\cong\; \mathbf 1_V ,
  \]
  the strong-monoidal \(\varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}\) inverse (not the bare lax \(\varepsilon\) of
  \cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_eps}).
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Carrier-value of the pushforward comparator: $q$ is the $f.\mathtt{appIso}$-conjugation on carriers]
  \label{lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.pushforwardBetaUnitEpsAppOne}
  \uses{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal, def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso, lem:pushforward_mu_appiso_collapse, lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  This is the unit-side analogue of the tensorator collapse
  \cref{lem:pushforward_mu_appiso_collapse}. With \(f : Y \to X\) an open immersion,
  \(\beta\) the (sectionwise-bijective) structure-ring comparison, and
  \(q = \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,f = \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,
  \beta)^{-1}\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}), the sectionwise carrier action of
  \(q.\mathrm{hom} = \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)^{-1}\) at an
  \emph{arbitrary} carrier element \(y\) is, over a section \(V\),
  \[
    \bigl(q.\mathrm{hom}\bigr)_V (y)
      \;=\; (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{hom}\,(y) ,
  \]
  the \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)-conjugation of \(y\). The unit case \(y = 1\) is the motivating
  instance; the general form is the one the L1\('\) consumer
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural} requires (it instantiates at a non-unit
  carrier).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal, def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso, lem:pushforward_mu_appiso_collapse, lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}
  \leanok
  We compute the carrier action of \(q.\mathrm{hom} = \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,
  \beta)^{-1}\) at an arbitrary carrier element \(y\) by splitting the strong-monoidal structure of
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta\) along the factorization
  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta = \mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}} \,\circ\,
  \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta\) furnished by
  \cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal}. The argument is cleanest run on the \emph{inverse}
  \(q.\mathrm{inv} = \varepsilon(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)\): under \(\mathtt{Functor.Monoidal.comp}\)
  the lax unit comparator \(\varepsilon\) of the composite is the composite of the two factors'
  \(\varepsilon\)'s, and the scalar-forgetting pushforward \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}}\)
  has \(\varepsilon = \mathrm{id}\), so \(q.\mathrm{inv}\) reduces to the \(\varepsilon\)-value of
  \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\beta\). That value is the sectionwise structure-ring transport
  \((f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{inv}\), by the abstract \(\varepsilon\)-template
  \cref{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal_eps_app} (the strong-monoidal companion of the oplax
  \(\eta\)-template \cref{lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}; \(\varepsilon = \eta^{-1}\)
  since \(\beta\) is sectionwise bijective). Hence
  \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{inv}\bigr)_V (w) = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{inv}\,(w)\)
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso_inv_app}). Now both \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{hom}\bigr)_V(y)\) and
  \((f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{hom}\,(y)\) are sent to \(y\) by the injective
  \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{inv}\bigr)_V\) — the former by the round-trip \(q.\mathrm{hom}\,;q.\mathrm{inv} =
  \mathrm{id}\), the latter by \(\mathtt{appIso}\)'s hom-inv identity — so they are equal:
  \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{hom}\bigr)_V (y) = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{hom}\,(y)\), the
  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)-conjugation of \(y\). This isolates the same structure-ring transport that the
  tensorator collapse \cref{lem:pushforward_mu_appiso_collapse} isolates on the \(\mu\)-side.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Abstract $\varepsilon$-value of $\mathtt{restrictScalars}$ on carriers]
  \label{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal_eps_app}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.restrictScalars_laxMonoidal_ε_app}
  \uses{lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  For ring-presheaves \(R, S : \mathcal C^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathtt{CommRingCat}\) and a natural
  transformation \(\alpha : R \to S\), the lax unit comparator \(\varepsilon\) of
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\alpha\) acts sectionwise on carriers by the ring map
  \(\alpha\): over a section \(W\), \(\varepsilon.\mathrm{app}\,W\,(r) = (\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,W).\mathrm{hom}\,(r)\).
  This is the abstract \(\{R,S : \mathcal C^{\mathrm{op}} \to \mathtt{CommRingCat}\}\) companion of the
  unit-value template \cref{lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}; the abstract framing makes
  the ambient \(\mathtt{CommRing}\) instance native (the concrete post-\(\mathtt{forget}_2\) Scheme
  carrier exposes only \(\mathtt{Ring}\)).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:restrictscalars_oplaxmonoidal_eta_app_one}
  \leanok
  Unfold \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\)'s lax-monoidal \(\varepsilon\): it is, by construction,
  the scalar-action map induced by \(\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,W\) on the unit module, i.e. multiplication by
  \((\alpha.\mathrm{app}\,W).\mathrm{hom}\,(r)\). Reading off its value at a carrier element gives the
  claim directly.
\end{proof}
