\leanok
[Carrier-value of the pushforward comparator inverse $q.\mathrm{inv} = \varepsilon$]

  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafPushforwardUnitIso_inv_app}
  \uses{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso, lem:presheaf_pushforward_beta_monoidal, lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal_eps_app}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  In the open-immersion setting, with \(q = \mathtt{presheafPushforwardUnitIso}\,f\)

  \(\bigl(q.\mathrm{inv}\bigr)_V (w) = (f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V).\mathrm{inv}\,(w)\).

  Split \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta = \mathtt{pushforward}_0^{\mathrm{Comm}} \circ

      \(p := \mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,j : \mathtt{pullback}_\varphi\,\mathbf 1_U \cong \mathbf 1_V\)
      (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}), the H1-transport \((H_1.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_U)^{-1}
      \mathbin{;} q\) of the pushforward comparator;
    \item the pushforward-side comparator
      \(q := \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta)^{-1} :
        (\mathtt{pushforward}_\beta)\,\mathbf 1_U \cong \mathbf 1_V\)
      (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}), the inverse of the strong-monoidal unit comparator
      of the pushforward.
  \end{itemize}
  Modulo the eval-at-\(1\) dual-of-unit isomorphism \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}\)
  (\cref{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso}) on the \(U\)- and \(V\)-flanks, \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\)
  \emph{is} the dual-of-unit identification, with the two comparators on opposite flanks: \(q\) is
  transported contravariantly through \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\) onto the pushforward codomain of
  \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\), while \(p\) closes the pullback domain on the right:
  \[
    \theta_{\mathbf 1}
      \,\mathbin{;}\, \bigl(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,q\bigr)^{-1}
      \,\mathbin{;}\, \mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_V
    \;=\; \mathtt{pullback}_\varphi\bigl(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_U\bigr)
      \,\mathbin{;}\, p,
  \]
  both sides being isomorphisms
  \(\mathtt{pullback}_\varphi(\mathtt{dual}\,\mathbf 1_U) \cong \mathbf 1_V\). This is the
  unit-specialisation of the immersion-naturality \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural}
  and the immersion analogue of the unit-automorphism core
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison, lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural, lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality, lem:internal_hom_eval, def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso, def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso, def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso, lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one, lem:slice_dual_transport_app_apply, lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app}
  \leanok
  By element extensionality on dual sections, exactly as in
  \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural}, of which this is the unit specialisation. Both
  \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\) and \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}\) are morphisms of presheaves of modules:
  \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\) is the internal-hom base-change comparison
  \(f^{*}\mathcal{H}om(\mathbf 1, \mathcal{O}) \to \mathcal{H}om(f^{*}\mathbf 1, f^{*}\mathcal{O})\)
  (\cref{def:presheafdual_pullback_comparison}), and \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}\) is the
  sectionwise evaluation-at-\(1\) of the internal hom of the unit into itself
  (\cref{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso}). Testing the displayed equality against an arbitrary dual
  section reduces, as in \cref{lem:presheafdual_pullback_restrict_natural}, to the built-in
  naturality of \(\theta\) (its \(\mathtt{.hom}\) naturality), now read through the eval-at-\(1\)
  identification, where the internal-hom evaluation (\cref{lem:internal_hom_eval}) collapses both
  composites to evaluation of the pulled-back section at the image of \(1\).

  The two flanks carry the two \emph{different} comparators. On the pushforward codomain of
  \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\) the comparator \(q = \varepsilon_{\mathrm{iso}}(\mathtt{pushforward}\,
  \beta)^{-1}\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pushforward_unit_iso}) is transported contravariantly by
  \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\); on the pullback domain the comparator
  \(p = \mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,j\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}) closes the
  square. Because \(p\) is, by definition, the H1-transport \((H_1.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_U)^{-1}
  \mathbin{;} q\), the two leading H1 factors --- the one hidden inside \(p\) and the one produced by
  unfolding \(\theta_{\mathbf 1}\) at the dual unit --- are telescoped away by the naturality of the
  natural isomorphism \(H_1 : \mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathtt{pullback}\,
  \varphi\) at the dual-unit contraction \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_U\)
  (\cref{lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app}). After this cancellation the entire claim reduces to a
  single \emph{pushforward-flank} sectionwise identity ($\dagger$): writing \(\mathtt{sDT}\) for the
  slice-dual transport \(\mathtt{sliceDualTransport}\,f\,\mathbf 1_U\),
  \[
    \mathtt{sDT}
      \,\mathbin{;}\, \bigl(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,q\bigr)^{-1}
      \,\mathbin{;}\, \mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_V
    \;=\;
    (\mathtt{pushforward}_\beta)\bigl(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_U\bigr)
      \,\mathbin{;}\, q .
  \]

  This identity is proved sectionwise, as the immersion analogue of
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}. Fix a section \(V\) and a dual section \(\varphi\).
  \begin{itemize}
    \item \emph{Left-hand side.} The verified forward transport formula
      (\cref{lem:slice_dual_transport_app_apply}) evaluates \(\mathtt{sDT}\) at the terminal slice as
      the reindexing across \(f.\mathtt{opensFunctor}\) conjugated by \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\) and the
      dual-unit ring swap; precomposing by \(q\) and evaluating at \(1\) (the dual-unit iso
      \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso} = \mathtt{evalLin}\,(\cdot)\,1\),
      \cref{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso}) exposes \(\mathtt{evalLin}\,\mathbf 1_U\,(\mathrm{op}\,fV)\,
      \varphi\,1\) conjugated by \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\) and the dual-unit ring swap.
    \item \emph{Right-hand side.} The pushforward of the eval-at-\(1\) contraction
      \((\mathtt{pushforward}_\beta)(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}_U)\) followed by the unit comparator
      \(q.\mathrm{hom}\) is reduced by the unit-value sub-lemma
      \cref{lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one}: the carrier action of \(q.\mathrm{hom}\) at \(1\)
      is the \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)-conjugation of \(1\), so the right side reduces to the same
      \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\)-conjugated \(\mathtt{evalLin}\,\mathbf 1_U\,(\mathrm{op}\,fV)\,\varphi\,1\).
  \end{itemize}
  Both sides being the same eval-at-\(1\) value conjugated by \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\), equality is the
  commutativity of \(\mathcal{O}_V\)-linear endomorphisms of the (commutative) structure ring --- the
  same \(\mathtt{linearEndo\_apply\_comm}\)-style step that closes
  \cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}, now with \(p\) and \(q\) in place of the unit
  automorphism. Hence both sides of ($\dagger$) agree sectionwise, and with the H1 telescope this gives the displayed
  square.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}


\leanok
[S4a / L2$'$: unit assembly --- the dual-restriction comparison at the unit intertwines the dual-unit contractions]
  \label{lem:dual_unit_iso_restrict_assemble}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.dual_unit_iso_restrict_assemble}
  \uses{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural, lem:dual_restrict_iso, lem:dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison, lem:sheafificationcomppullback_restrict, lem:dual_unit_iso, def:unitrestrictiso, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso, lem:image_preimage_of_le, lem:forget_unit_restrict_eq_pushforward_unit, lem:pullback_unit_sheafify_reconcile}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}
  With \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) as above, write \(\mathbf 1_U, \mathbf 1_V\) for the structure
  sheaves, \(u_j = \mathtt{unitRestrictIso}\,j\) (\cref{def:unitrestrictiso}) for the chart-scheme
  unit identification \(\mathbf 1_U|_j \cong \mathbf 1_V\), and

  (\cref{lem:dual_unit_iso}). The \emph{bare} dual-restriction comparison
  \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,(\mathbf 1_U) : (\mathtt{dual}\,\mathbf 1_U)|_j \cong

        \,\mathbin{;}\, \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V,

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural, lem:dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison, lem:sheafificationcomppullback_restrict, lem:dual_unit_iso, def:unitrestrictiso, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso, lem:image_preimage_of_le, lem:forget_unit_restrict_eq_pushforward_unit, lem:pullback_unit_sheafify_reconcile}
  \leanok
  Drop both sides to their underlying morphisms (\(\mathtt{Iso.ext}\)) and flatten every
  \(\mathbin{;}\), \(\mathrm{mapIso}\) and inverse into a single right-associated composite. Expanding
  the bare comparison \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,(\mathbf 1_U)\) by the dual-B1 factorisation
  (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_eq_comparison}) into the three-factor composite
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,j \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,\varphi
  \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafification}(\theta_{\mathbf 1})\), and each contraction by its definition
  \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso} = \mathtt{sheafification}(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}) \mathbin{;}
  \mathtt{counit}\) (\cref{lem:dual_unit_iso}), turns the square into a purely syntactic identity of
  two right-associated \(\mathbin{;}\)-chains.

  This flattened identity is discharged by a verified \emph{category-algebra assembly}: an abstract
  reassociation lemma over a bare category, proven by associativity bookkeeping alone, that crosses
  the \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,\mathbin{;}\) seam without any unfolding. The assembly splits the
  left-hand unit-restriction map \(m\) as a prefix \(m_{\mathrm p}\) followed by a core
  \(m_{\mathrm c}\) and consumes four hypotheses --- a split identity, a flank identity, an
  scp-prefix identity, and a tail identity. The split, scp-prefix and tail data are folded
  \(\mathbin{;}\)-bookkeeping (the \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback} \mathbin{;}
  \mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) prefix is matched, exactly as in S3, by the composition law of
  bridge~(b), \cref{lem:sheafificationcomppullback_restrict}, threading the equality of opens
  \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\), \cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}; and the
  sheafification-counit naturality moves the counits past the restriction while contravariant
  functoriality of \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\), \cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}, collects the
  dual transport into \((\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,u_j)^{-1}\)). The whole \(\theta\)-at-unit
  identification supplied by L1$'$ (\cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_pullback_natural}) is consumed
  inside this fold. The substantive content of the four hypotheses reduces to exactly two residual
  reconciliations:

  \begin{itemize}
    \item the \emph{dual flank} (\cref{lem:forget_unit_restrict_eq_pushforward_unit}): the
      forgotten sheaf-level unit-restriction comparator equals the presheaf pushforward-unit
      comparator \(q\), which discharges the dual-flank leg of the assembly;
    \item the \emph{pullback (unit-only) flank} (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_sheafify_reconcile}): the
      sheaf pullback-unit comparator sheafifies the presheaf pullback-unit comparator \(p\), threaded
      through \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) and the two sheafification counits. After
      reduced-image/SCP-naturality and the common-prefix cancel, the residual is unit-only --- no
      dual factor remains.
  \end{itemize}

  Modulo these two reconciliations the square is closed by the folded assembly; the keystone is
  therefore sorry-free up to them. The genuine open work is precisely these two adjunction-coherence

  \cref{lem:unit_leftadjointuniq_hom_app}, \cref{lem:pushforward_beta_unit_eps_app_one}).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

  Let \(f : Y \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion. Pulling back the sheafification counit over
  \(X\) at the structure-sheaf unit and then applying the sheaf pullback-unit iso
  \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f\) equals: the \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\) comparison at the
  unit presheaf, then the sheafification of the presheaf pullback-unit comparator
  \(p = \mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,f\) (\cref{def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso}), then the
  sheafification counit over \(Y\) at the unit:
  \[
    (\mathtt{pullback}\,f).\mathrm{map}\bigl(\mathtt{sheafCounit}_X.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_X\bigr)
      \;\mathbin{;}\; (\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom}
      \;=\; \mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,\varphi.\mathrm{hom}.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_X^{\mathrm p}
  \]
  \[
      \qquad\qquad
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{sheafification}.\mathrm{map}\bigl((\mathtt{presheafPullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\bigr)
        \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{sheafCounit}_Y.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_Y ,
  \]
  where \(\mathtt{sheafCounit}_\bullet\) is the counit of the sheafification adjunction over
  \(\bullet\) and \(\mathbf 1_X^{\mathrm p}\) the underlying presheaf unit. This is the genuine
  unit-side reconciliation of the dual-unit naturality square; no dual factor remains.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:sheafification_comp_pullback_eq_leftadjointuniq, def:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso, lem:pullback_unit_iso, lem:pullback_val_iso_unit_factor, lem:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso_sheafify_reconcile}
  \leanok
  Rewrite the right-hand comparator leg
  \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(p.\mathrm{hom}) \mathbin{;} \mathtt{cuY}.\mathrm{app}\,\mathbf 1_Y\) by L2
  (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_unit_iso_sheafify_reconcile}), then expand
  \((\mathtt{pullbackValIso}\,f\,M_0).\mathrm{hom}\) by L1
  (\cref{lem:pullback_val_iso_unit_factor}); the leading
  \(\mathtt{sheafificationCompPullback}\,\varphi\) hom/inv legs cancel
  (\(\mathtt{Iso.hom\_inv\_id\_app}\)), leaving the left-hand side
  \((\mathtt{pullback}\,f).\mathrm{map}(\mathtt{cuX}.\mathrm{app}\,M_0) \mathbin{;}
  (\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\). All seam-crossing mate-calculus is confined to L2.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Localization-monoidal left-unitor collapse]
  \label{lem:localization_monoidal_left_unitor_hom_app}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.leftUnitor_hom_app}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(L : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}\) be a localization functor for a monoidal morphism property
  \(W\), so that \(\mathcal{D} = \mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\,L\,W\,\varepsilon\) carries the transported
  (Gabriel--Zisman / Day-style) monoidal structure, with unit comparison \(\varepsilon\) and tensor
  comparison \(\mu_{X,Y} : L X \otimes L Y \cong L(X \otimes Y)\). For every object \(Y\) of
  \(\mathcal{C}\), the localized left unitor at the image object \(L Y\) collapses to the \(L\)-image of
  the source left unitor, modulo the unit and tensor comparisons:
  \[
    (\lambda_{L Y}).\mathrm{hom}
      = \varepsilon^{-1} \rhd L Y
        \mathbin{;} (\mu_{\mathbf 1,\,Y}).\mathrm{hom}
        \mathbin{;} L.\mathrm{map}\,(\lambda_Y).\mathrm{hom},
  \]
  where \(\mathbf 1\) is the source tensor unit. The proof is definitional: the localized left unitor
  is constructed as a \(\mathtt{Localization.liftNatIso}\) of the source unitor, so on an image object it
  reduces by \cref{lem:localization_lift_nat_trans_app} to the source datum conjugated by the lifting
  isomorphisms, with no further coherence obligations.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[Evaluation of a lifted natural transformation on an image object]
  \label{lem:localization_lift_nat_trans_app}
  \lean{CategoryTheory.Localization.liftNatTrans_app}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(L : \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{D}\) be a localization functor for \(W\), let \(F_1, F_2 :
  \mathcal{C} \to \mathcal{E}\) be functors that lift to \(F_1', F_2' : \mathcal{D} \to \mathcal{E}\)
  along \(L\), and let \(\tau : F_1 \to F_2\). The unique lifted natural transformation
  \(\mathtt{liftNatTrans}\,\tau : F_1' \to F_2'\), evaluated at an image object \(L X\), is the source
  datum conjugated by the lifting isomorphisms:
  \[
    (\mathtt{liftNatTrans}\,\tau)_{L X}
      = (\mathtt{Lifting.iso}\,F_1')_{X}.\mathrm{hom}
        \mathbin{;} \tau_X
        \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{Lifting.iso}\,F_2')_{X}.\mathrm{inv}.
  \]
  Thus a coherence between lifted transformations, tested on objects in the image of \(L\), reduces
  definitionally to the corresponding coherence of the source data pinned by the lifting iso --- the
  mechanism underlying the localization-monoidal unitor collapse above.
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}[The sheafification of presheaves of modules is a localization]
  \label{lem:presheaf_sheafification_is_localization}
  \lean{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}
  \mathlibok
  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
  Let \(\alpha : R_0 \to R.\mathrm{obj}\) be the comparison from a presheaf of rings to its
  sheafification (locally injective and locally surjective). The sheafification reflector
  \(a = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\,\alpha : \mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R_0 \to
  \mathtt{SheafOfModules}\,R\) is a localization functor for the morphism class
  \(W = J.W.\mathtt{inverseImage}\,(\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R_0)\) of presheaf morphisms inverted by
  sheafification. Consequently the entire \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal}\) API ---
  \cref{lem:localization_monoidal_left_unitor_hom_app} and
  \cref{lem:localization_lift_nat_trans_app} in particular --- is applicable to \(a\).
\end{lemma}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[STU-collapse: the sheaf left unitor at a pullback image object]
  \label{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_image_collapse}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor_image_collapse}
  % NOTE (iter-092 review, lvb minor): the display below orients the tensor comparison as
  % \((\mu_{\mathbf 1,\cdot}).\mathrm{hom}\) in the solved-for-unitor form, but solving the authoritative
  % Lean statement \(\mu.\mathrm{hom};(\varepsilon\rhd);\mathrm{unitor}=a.\mathrm{map}\,\lambda\) for the
  % unitor yields \(\mu.\mathbf{inv}\) in the middle factor, not \(\mu.\mathrm{hom}\). The Lean statement
  % is correct and was formalized; only this paraphrase has the hom/inv slip. Cosmetic; non-blocking.
  \uses{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_hom_eq_prime, def:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  Let \(f : Y \to X\), write \(a = a_Y = \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\) for the
  sheafification reflector on \(Y\), \(\varepsilon = \mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\) for its unit comparison
  and \(\mu = \mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) for its tensor comparison, and \(F = \mathtt{pullback}\,
  \varphi'\) for the presheaf-level change-of-rings pullback. For the image object
  \(a.\mathrm{obj}\,(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})\) --- the sheafification of the presheaf pullback
  of \(M\) --- the sheaf-level left unitor collapses, modulo \(\varepsilon\) and \(\mu\), to the
  sheafified presheaf left unitor:
  \[
    \bigl(\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\,(a.\mathrm{obj}\,(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}))\bigr).\mathrm{hom}
      = \varepsilon^{-1} \rhd a.\mathrm{obj}\,(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})
        \mathbin{;} (\mu_{\mathbf 1,\,F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}).\mathrm{hom}
        \mathbin{;} a.\mathrm{map}\,\bigl(\lambda_{F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}\bigr).
  \]
  This is the shared structural residual of \cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_pullback_eq_sheafify} and
  of the central-leg sub-step of
  \cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso_whisker_eq_sheafify_eta_whisker}.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_hom_eq_prime, def:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  \leanok
  The proof is a by-hand presheaf reduction across the sheafification adjunction; it does \emph{not}
  use the localization-monoidal API. The genuine content is a tensor-bifunctoriality plus
  adjunction-triangle collapse over the \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,(\mathbf 1)\) / \(\mathbf 1_{\mathtt{Functor}}\)
  defeq seam, not a definitional pass-through.

  \emph{Step 1: single-tensor presentation.} Present
  \(\mu = \mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) by its single-tensor form
  (\cref{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_hom_eq_prime}, \cref{def:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso}):
  \(\mu.\mathrm{hom} = a.\mathrm{map}(\eta_{\mathbf 1} \otimes \eta_P)\), where
  \(\eta = (\mathtt{sheafificationAdjunction}).\mathrm{unit}\) is the sheafification unit and \(a\) the
  reflector; and expand \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\) at the image object as the sheafified
  presheaf left unitor (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}). All factors are kept inside the one
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) monoidal instance on the \(\circ\,\mathtt{forget}_2\) carrier.

  \emph{Step 2: presheaf collapse identity.} Reduce the goal to the pure presheaf equation
  \[
    (\eta_{\mathbf 1} \otimes \eta_P) \mathbin{;} (\varepsilon \otimes \mathbf 1) \mathbin{;} \lambda_{aP}
      = \lambda_P \mathbin{;} \eta_P ,
  \]
  obtained from tensor bifunctoriality (the interchange law
  \((f_1\otimes f_2)\circ(g_1\otimes g_2) = (f_1\circ g_1)\otimes(f_2\circ g_2)\)), the
  sheafification-adjunction \emph{right-triangle} \(\eta_{\mathbf 1}\mathbin{;}\varepsilon = \mathbf 1\)
  on the unit factor, and presheaf left-unitor naturality of \(\eta_P\).

  \emph{Step 3: merge the functor-image legs and cancel the counit.} Both sides are
  \(a.\mathrm{map}\)-images; merge them by functoriality \(F(g)\circ F(h)=F(g\circ h)\), apply the
  Step-2 identity inside \(a.\mathrm{map}\), and cancel the trailing counit by the sheafification
  adjunction \emph{left-triangle}. This leaves exactly the displayed equation. The merge, the
  triangle rewrites, and the unit-factor whisker are all forced to be term-mode
  (\(\mathtt{congrArg}_2\)/\(\mathtt{Eq.trans}\)) because the \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,(\mathbf 1)\)
  wrapper on \(\eta\)'s codomain makes the connecting objects defeq-but-not-syntactic, so
  \(\mathtt{rw}\)/\(\mathtt{erw}\) miss or \(\mathtt{whnf}\)-bomb.
\end{proof}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_naturality, lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_image_collapse, def:pullback_val_iso}
  \leanok
  Split the wrapper's tensor leg \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso} \otimes (\mathtt{pbv}\,M))\)
  by functoriality of the sheaf tensor into the unit-factor whisker
  \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso} \rhd (-)\) followed by the right-factor whisker
  \((-) \lhd (\mathtt{pbv}\,M)\). Naturality of the sheaf-level left unitor against the
  \(\mathtt{pbv}\,M\)-leg (\cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_naturality}) slides that right-factor whisker
  past the unitor, producing the trailing \((\mathtt{pbv}_{M}).\mathrm{hom}\) on the right-hand side and
  reducing the unitor to its value at the image object
  \(a_Y.\mathrm{obj}\,(F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})\). The residual --- the
  \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\) reconciliation \(\mu\) and the \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\)
  whisker \(\varepsilon\) composed against the sheaf left unitor at that image object --- is exactly the
  localization-image collapse \cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_image_collapse}: the sheaf left unitor at
  the image object collapses, modulo \(\varepsilon\) and \(\mu\), to
  \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\lambda_{F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}})\). Cancelling the
  \(\varepsilon\)-whisker against the collapse's \(\varepsilon^{-1}\)-whisker leaves the displayed
  identity. No new sheaf-level monoidal structure is introduced --- only unitor naturality and the
  image-collapse \cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_image_collapse}, whose own proof is the by-hand
  bifunctoriality + adjunction-triangle reduction recorded there.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:pullback_unit_iso_eq_sheafify_eta, lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal, lem:pullback_tensor_map_eq_sheafify_delta, lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_natural, def:pullback_val_iso}
  \leanok
  The proof composes two reconciliations.

  \emph{Bridge-1 substitution and whisker expansion.} The whisker
  \((\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f)\,\mathbf 1_{f^{*}M}).\mathrm{hom}\) is the
  bifunctorial action of the sheaf tensor on \((\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\) and the
  identity. Substituting bridge~1 (\cref{lem:pullback_unit_iso_eq_sheafify_eta}),
  \((\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,f).\mathrm{hom} = (\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X).\mathrm{inv}
  \mathbin{;} a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\eta\,F) \mathbin{;} \mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}.\mathrm{hom}\), and
  splitting by functoriality of the tensor product expresses the whisker as the three whiskered legs
  \((\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X).\mathrm{inv}\), \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\eta\,F)\),
  \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}.\mathrm{hom}\), each whiskered by \(\mathbf 1_{f^{*}M}\) into the left
  factor.

  \emph{Central-leg STU naturality.} The central leg --- \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\eta\,F)\) whiskered by
  \(f^{*}M\), transported across the tensor reconciliation \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\)
  identifying the sheaf tensor with the sheafification of the presheaf tensor
  \(F.\mathrm{obj}\,\mathbf 1 \otimes F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}\) --- becomes the bare sheafified
  presheaf \(\eta\)-whisker \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\eta\,F \rhd F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})\). This is
  \emph{not} a definitional pass-through: it is exactly the argument-1 specialization, at
  \(q = \mathbf 1_{F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}}}\), of the \emph{already-proven} two-argument
  naturality \cref{lem:sheafify_tensor_unit_iso_natural} of \(\mathtt{sheafifyTensorUnitIso}\):
  \[
    a_Y.\mathrm{map}(p \otimes \mathbf 1) \mathbin{;} \mathtt{STU}(P',Q)

  \]
  instantiated at \(p = \eta\,F\). The general lemma's right-hand whisker
  \((a_Y.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathbf 1))_{\mathrm{val}}\) collapses to the identity by
  \(\mathtt{Functor.map\_id}\) / \(\mathtt{tensorHom\_id}\), so no new coherence core has to be built ---
  the proven section-level naturality discharges this leg directly.

  \emph{Left-factor device cancellation.} The flanking legs
  \((\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X).\mathrm{inv}\) and \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}.\mathrm{hom}\)
  introduced by the substitution, whiskered into the left factor, recombine with the wrapper \(W\). The
  \((\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X)\)-component of \(W\)'s tensor leg is inverse to
  \((\mathtt{pbv}\,\mathcal{O}_X).\mathrm{inv}\), so it cancels and rewrites \(W\)'s tensor unit
  \(F\,\mathbf 1\) to the presheaf unit \(\mathbf 1\) and its left leg to \(\mathtt{sheafifyUnitIso}\),
  i.e. converts \(W\) into the unit-reconciled wrapper \(W_{\lambda}\) (\cref{def:pullback_val_iso}).
  Reassociating exposes the cancelling pairs (\(\mathtt{Iso.hom\_inv\_id}\)/\(\mathtt{inv\_hom\_id}\)),
  and the composite is exactly \(a_Y.\mathrm{map}(\eta\,F \rhd F.\mathrm{obj}\,M_{\mathrm{val}})
  \mathbin{;} W_{\lambda}\), the displayed identity. This is the unit-side analogue of the whiskering
  for the unit pair in \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_iso_unit}, now retaining the whisker rather than
  discarding it as an isomorphism.
\end{proof}

  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_pullback_eq_sheafify}), and the right-hand side to the

\begin{lemma}



\leanok
[S4b: unit left-unitor commutes with further restriction]
  \label{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso_restrict_compat}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_unit_iso_restrict_compat}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:image_preimage_of_le}
  With \(j : V \hookrightarrow U\) as above, the unit-tensor contraction
  \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso} : \mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U (-) \cong (-)\) (the left unitor at the
  unit, \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}) commutes with further restriction along \(j\): modulo the
  reindexing \(\varrho\),
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}\ \text{over}\ U)
      \;=\; \mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}\ \text{over}\ V.
  \]
  Unlike the tensor-restriction comparison \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_restrict\_iso}\), the contraction
  \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}\) is \emph{not} built from the abstract pullback: by definition it
  is the \emph{sheafification of the presheaf-level left unitor at the unit}, followed by the
  sheafification-adjunction counit (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}). Bridge B1 does not apply to the
  contraction directly; instead the square reduces, via the chart comparison
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\), to a pullback-world left-unitality coherence (Cone~A,
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_left_unitality}), established below.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso_eq_left_unitor, lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_naturality, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso_restrict_compat_inner, lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal, def:unitrestrictiso, def:restrictcompreindex, lem:image_preimage_of_le}
  \leanok
  This is the \emph{unit} companion of S2, routed through \emph{Cone~A}. Because the unit object is
  fixed --- there is no module argument --- the square has \emph{no} naturality-in-\(M\) component,
  which makes it strictly simpler than B1.

  \emph{Peeling the contraction.} Write
  \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso} = \mathtt{sheafification.mapIso}(\lambda_{\mathbf 1})
  \mathbin{;} \varepsilon\), where \(\lambda_{\mathbf 1}\) is the presheaf-level left unitor of
  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) at the tensor unit \(\mathbf 1 = \mathcal{O}\) and \(\varepsilon\) is
  the sheafification-adjunction counit. By \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso_eq_left_unitor} the
  contraction is the sheaf-level left unitor \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_left\_unitor}\)
  (\cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}) evaluated at \(M = \mathcal{O}\); restriction along \(j\) commutes
  through the outer \(\mathtt{Functor.mapIso}\)/\(\mathtt{toSheafify}\)/counit legs by functoriality
  and naturality of the unitor (\cref{lem:tensorobj_left_unitor_naturality}), so the square reduces to
  a single \emph{inner} seam.

  \emph{Inner seam (Cone~A).} The inner seam is the pullback-world left-unitor coherence at the chart
  unit, \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso_restrict_compat_inner}. Crucially, this is \emph{not} a missing
  monoidal instance: the presheaf change-of-rings pullback \(\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi'\) is
  \emph{already} oplax monoidal (\cref{lem:presheaf_pullback_oplaxmonoidal}, via
  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal}\)), so its left-unitality coherence
  \(\mathtt{Functor.OplaxMonoidal.left\_unitality\_hom}\) holds for free. The inner seam is the
  \emph{sheafification transport} of that coherence to the sheaf-level maps
  \(\mathtt{pullbackTensorMap}\) (sheafified \(\delta\)) and \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\) (sheafified
  \(\eta\)); it is discharged by the sheaf-level left unitality
  \cref{lem:pullback_tensor_map_left_unitality} at \(M = \mathcal{O}_U\), through the chart comparison
  \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\,j\) and the unit conjugator \(\mathtt{unitRestrictIso}\,j\)
  (\cref{def:unitrestrictiso}). The pentagon plays no role.

  \emph{Outer seam (sheafify and counit).} The remaining legs are formal. The sheafification functor
  acts on the inner unitor square by \(\mathtt{Functor.mapIso}\), and the sheafification unit
  \(\mathtt{toSheafify}\) is natural, so the sheafified squares commute whenever the inner one does.
  The counit \(\varepsilon\) of the sheafification adjunction is a natural transformation, hence its
  naturality square against the restriction-induced morphism is free. Composing the inner agreement
  with these two formal squares yields the equality of the two contractions.

  \emph{Reindexing.} Finally the reindexing \(\varrho = \mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\)
  (\cref{def:restrictcompreindex}) threads the equality of opens
  \(j \mathbin{;} \iota_U = \iota_V\) (\cref{lem:image_preimage_of_le}) through both flanks, absorbing
  the \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\) congruence exactly as in S2, and giving the displayed
  identity. No sheaf-level monoidal structure is involved: the structure used lives at the presheaf
  level and is transported through sheafification.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok

  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}

  \uses{def:restrictfunctorisopullback}

  \[
    \mathtt{RFIP}(\iota_V)
      \mathbin{;} \mathtt{pullbackCongr}(h_{j\iota})
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{pullbackComp}\,j\,\iota_U)^{-1}
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{pullback}\,j)\bigl(\mathtt{RFIP}(\iota_U)^{-1}\bigr)
      \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{pullback}\,j)\,e^M
      \mathbin{;} \mathtt{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\,j,
  \]
  where \(\mathtt{RFIP} = \mathtt{restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\). On the right-hand side,
  \(\mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\) unfolds (\cref{def:restrictcompreindex}) as
  \((\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\,h_{j\iota})^{-1} \mathbin{;}
  \mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\,j\,\iota_U\), and \(\mathtt{unitRestrictIso}\,j\) unfolds
  (\cref{def:unitrestrictiso}) as
  \(\mathtt{RFIP}(j).\mathrm{app}\,\mathcal{O}_U \mathbin{;} \mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,j\). Pushing the
  two restriction-world factors \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorComp}\) and \(\mathtt{restrictFunctorCongr}\)
  into the pullback world by the general pseudonaturality bridge
  (\cref{lem:restrictfunctorisopullback_comp_general}) turns the right-hand side into the same
  pullback-world chart-chase as the left, the trailing \(\mathtt{pullbackObjUnitToUnit}\,j\) matching
  \(\mathtt{pullbackUnitIso}\,j\) definitionally. The two presentations therefore agree.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

\leanok
[Telescope seam 1, Step C: the dual-chain leg, distributed and identified leg by leg]
  \label{lem:trivialisation_dual_chain_leg}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.trivialisation_restrict_dual_leg}
  % NOTE: Lean decl `trivialisation_restrict_dual_leg` not yet created (dangling \lean{} target —

  \uses{lem:dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat, lem:dual_restrict_iso_natural, lem:dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat, lem:restrict_iso_unit_of_le_eq_restrict, lem:dualisoofiso_trans, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso, def:restrictcompreindex, def:unitrestrictiso}
  \textit{Source: internal categorical construction; no external reference.}

  \(c = \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_U \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e^M)^{-1}
  \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U\) for the \(U\)-built dual chain and
  \[
    c_V = \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_V
      \mathbin{;} \bigl(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,(\mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\,h_{VU}\,e^M)\bigr)^{-1}
      \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V

  for its \(V\)-built refinement (with \(e^M\) replaced by \(\mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\,h_{VU}\,e^M\)).
  Writing \(\varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,L} = \mathtt{restrictCompReindex}\,j\,(\mathtt{dual}\,L)\) and
  \(u_j = \mathtt{unitRestrictIso}\,j\), the \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\)-image of the dual chain is its
  \(V\)-built refinement conjugated by \(\varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,L}\) and \(u_j\):
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,j).\mathrm{mapIso}\,c

\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat, lem:dual_restrict_iso_natural, lem:dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat, lem:restrict_iso_unit_of_le_eq_restrict, lem:dualisoofiso_trans, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}
  \leanok
  As \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\) is a functor it carries the composite \(c\) to the composite of the
  \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\)-images of its three legs; identify each in turn (abbreviating
  \(D_j(X) = \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,j\,X\)).
  \begin{itemize}
    \item \emph{Leg 1}, \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_U\): S3
      (\cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat}) reads
      \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_V = \varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,L}
        \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_U)
        \mathbin{;} D_j(L|_{\iota_U}) \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\varrho_L\);
      solving for the middle factor gives
      \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_U)
        = \varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,L}^{-1} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_V
          \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\varrho_L)^{-1} \mathbin{;} D_j(L|_{\iota_U})^{-1}\).
    \item \emph{Leg 2}, \((\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e^M)^{-1}\): module-naturality of
      \(\mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\) (T2, \cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_natural}) reads
      \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e^M)
        = D_j(\mathcal O_U) \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,e^M)
          \mathbin{;} D_j(L|_{\iota_U})^{-1}\);
      taking inverses, the \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\)-image of leg 2 is
      \(D_j(L|_{\iota_U}) \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,e^M))^{-1}
        \mathbin{;} D_j(\mathcal O_U)^{-1}\).
    \item \emph{Leg 3}, \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U\): S4a
      (\cref{lem:dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat}) reads
      \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V = \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,u_j \mathbin{;} D_j(\mathbf 1_U)^{-1}
        \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U \mathbin{;} u_j\);
      solving gives \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_U
        = D_j(\mathbf 1_U) \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,u_j)^{-1}
          \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V \mathbin{;} u_j^{-1}\).
  \end{itemize}
  Composing the three legs in order, the two internal \(D_j\) comparisons cancel telescopically: the
  tail \(D_j(L|_{\iota_U})^{-1}\) of leg 1 meets the head \(D_j(L|_{\iota_U})\) of leg 2, and the tail
  \(D_j(\mathcal O_U)^{-1}\) of leg 2 meets the head \(D_j(\mathbf 1_U) = D_j(\mathcal O_U)\) of leg 3
  (the structure-sheaf unit \(\mathbf 1_U\) being \(\mathcal O_U\)). What survives is
  \(\varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,L}^{-1} \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_restrict\_iso}\,\iota_V \mathbin{;} (\ast)
  \mathbin{;} \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}_V \mathbin{;} u_j^{-1}\), where the middle factor
  \((\ast) = (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\varrho_L)^{-1} \mathbin{;}
  (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,e^M))^{-1} \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,u_j)^{-1}\)
  is, by contravariant functoriality of \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\) (\cref{lem:dualisoofiso_trans},
  \cref{def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso}),
  \(\bigl(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,(\varrho_L \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,e^M \mathbin{;} u_j)\bigr)^{-1}\).
  Since \(\varrho_L \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,e^M \mathbin{;} u_j
    = \mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\,h_{VU}\,e^M\) is exactly the Seam-1 keystone
  (\cref{lem:restrict_iso_unit_of_le_eq_restrict}), \((\ast)\) is the middle leg
  \((\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,(\mathtt{restrictIsoUnitOfLE}\,h_{VU}\,e^M))^{-1}\) of \(c_V\), and the
  surviving expression is \(\varrho_{\mathtt{dual}\,L}^{-1} \mathbin{;} c_V \mathbin{;} u_j^{-1}\) as claimed.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}

[Telescope seam 2: generic \(\varrho\)-cancellation assembly of the five squares]

  internal reindexings \(\varrho_1, \dots, \varrho_4\) (the

  \cref{def:restrictcompreindex}), and the two outer reindexings \(\varrho_0, \varrho_5\), suppose each
  constituent satisfies its restriction-naturality square
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,c^U_k \;=\; \varrho_{k-1}^{-1} \mathbin{;} c^V_k \mathbin{;} \varrho_k
    \qquad (k = 1, \dots, 5),
  \]
  so that the target \(\varrho_k\) of square \(k\) is the source \(\varrho_k\) of square \(k+1\). Then,
  composing the five squares in order, the internal reindexings \(\varrho_1, \dots, \varrho_4\) cancel
  telescopically and
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\bigl(c^U_1 \mathbin{;} \cdots \mathbin{;} c^U_5\bigr)
      \;=\; \varrho_0^{-1} \mathbin{;} \bigl(c^V_1 \mathbin{;} \cdots \mathbin{;} c^V_5\bigr) \mathbin{;} \varrho_5,

  leaving only the two outer reindexings \(\varrho_0 = \mathtt{hobjU}\) and \(\varrho_5 = \mathtt{hobjV}\).
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}

\leanok
  Pure \(\mathtt{Category.assoc}\) cocycle collapse, identical in form to the sibling assembles
  \(\mathtt{dual\_scp\_assemble}\), \(\mathtt{unit\_assemble}\) and \(\mathtt{hcore\_assemble}\). As
  \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\) is a functor it carries the \(U\)-composite to the composite of the
  \(\mathtt{restrict}\,j\)-images; substitute each square, and for every adjacent pair the factor
  \(\varrho_k \mathbin{;} \varrho_k^{-1} = \mathbf{1}\) (\(k = 1, \dots, 4\)) cancels by the iso law.
  What survives is \(\varrho_0^{-1}\) on the left and \(\varrho_5\) on the right --- the displayed
  identity. No \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules} \mathbin{;}\) defeq seam is crossed: the statement lives over an
  abstract \([\mathtt{Category}\ C]\) and is applied to the concrete chain by \(\mathtt{exact}\), never
  \(\mathtt{rw}\)/\(\mathtt{ext}\) on a conjugate-headed goal.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}





\leanok
[Trivialisation iso-chain compatible with further restriction (cocycle residual A, step 1)]
  \label{lem:trivialisation_restrict_compat}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.trivialisation_restrict_compat}
  \uses{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_restrict_compat, lem:dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat, lem:dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso_restrict_compat, lem:trivialisation_uiota_restrict_compat, lem:restrictfunctorisopullback_comp_compat, def:restrictcompreindex, lem:image_preimage_of_le, lem:trivialisation_restrict_em_split, lem:trivialisation_telescope_assemble, lem:trivialisation_restrict_sectionwise}
  Let \(V \subseteq U\) be opens of \(X\) (an inclusion of trivialising opens, e.g.
  \(V \subseteq U_i \cap U_j\)). The trivialisation iso-chain that builds the local
  contraction --- the restriction-compatibility iso \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
  carrying \((L \otimes_X L^{-1})|_U\) to \(\mathcal{O}_U \otimes_U \mathcal{O}_U\),
  composed with the unit contraction \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso} --- is \emph{natural in
  the open}: its further restriction along \(V \hookrightarrow U\) equals the same chain
  built directly over \(V\). Consequently the sectionwise overlap equation of two local
  contractions \(f_i, f_j\) restricted to \(V\) lifts to an equation of \emph{restricted
  sheaf morphisms} over \(V\), the form on which the iso-level functoriality laws
  (\cref{lem:tensorobjisoofiso_trans}, \cref{lem:dualisoofiso_trans}) and
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_self_duality_collapse} can act.

\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:trivialisation_restrict_em_split, lem:trivialisation_telescope_assemble, lem:trivialisation_restrict_sectionwise, lem:tensorobjisoofiso_trans, lem:dualisoofiso_trans}
  \leanok
  The trivialisation chain carrying \((L \otimes_X L^{-1})|_U\) to \(\mathcal{O}_U\), then the global-unit
  comparison \(u_\iota(\iota_U)^{-1}\), is --- in order --- the five constituents S2--S4c, each carrying
  its own restriction-naturality square (\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso_restrict_compat},
  \cref{lem:dual_restrict_iso_restrict_compat}, \cref{lem:dual_unit_iso_restrict_compat},
  \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_iso_restrict_compat}, \cref{lem:trivialisation_uiota_restrict_compat}). The
  assembly is the three-seam chain of this section. Seam~1
  (\cref{lem:trivialisation_restrict_em_split}) handles the \((\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,e^M)^{-1}\)
  transport the trivialisation step wraps around constituent~(2): bifunctoriality of
  \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\) splits it into the \(e^M\)-leg \((\mathtt{restrict}\,j)\,e^M\) and the
  dual-chain leg covered by S3/S4a. Seam~2 (\cref{lem:trivialisation_telescope_assemble}) is the generic
  abstract-\([\mathtt{Category}]\) cocycle collapse: composing the five squares, the internal \(\varrho\)
  reindexings cancel telescopically, leaving only the outer
  \(\mathtt{hobjU} = \mathtt{image\_preimage\_of\_le}\,U\,h_{VU}\) and
  \(\mathtt{hobjV} = \mathtt{image\_preimage\_of\_le}\,V\,\mathrm{le\_rfl}\). Seam~3
  (\cref{lem:trivialisation_restrict_sectionwise}) threads those two outer \(\mathtt{eqToHom}\)s and
  evaluates the chain sectionwise over the preimage open \(\iota_U^{-1}(V)\), which is the asserted
  equality. Restricting the sectionwise overlap equation of two local contractions \(f_i, f_j\) through
  this compatibility lifts it to an equation of restricted sheaf morphisms over \(V\), the form on which
  the iso-level functoriality laws (\cref{lem:tensorobjisoofiso_trans}, \cref{lem:dualisoofiso_trans})
  and \cref{lem:tensorobj_unit_self_duality_collapse} act.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}




\leanok
[Mutually-inverse unit endomorphisms cancel against the left unitor]
  \label{lem:tensorhom_inv_comp_leftunitor}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorHom_inv_comp_leftUnitor}
  In any monoidal category \(\mathcal C\), if \(s, s' : \mathbf 1 \to \mathbf 1\) are endomorphisms
  of the monoidal unit with \(s \mathbin{;} s' = \mathbf 1\), then
  \[
    (s \otimes_{\mathrm m} s') \;\mathbin{;}\; \lambda_{\mathbf 1} = \lambda_{\mathbf 1},
  \]
  where \(\lambda_{\mathbf 1} : \mathbf 1 \otimes \mathbf 1 \to \mathbf 1\) is the left unitor at the
  unit.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}

\leanok
  Factor the tensor product \(s \otimes_{\mathrm m} s'\) through the interchange law. Sliding the
  right factor past the left unitor by its naturality, and the left factor past the right unitor
  (which coincides with the left unitor at the unit), the composite becomes \(\lambda_{\mathbf 1}\)
  precomposed with \(s \mathbin{;} s' = \mathbf 1\); cancelling that identity leaves
  \(\lambda_{\mathbf 1}\).
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}




\leanok
[Pairing mutually-inverse unit autos through $\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}$ contracts (cocycle residual A, step 2b)]
  \label{lem:tensorobjisoofiso_comp_unit_iso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObjIsoOfIso_comp_unit_iso}
  \uses{lem:tensorhom_inv_comp_leftunitor, def:tensorobjisoofiso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  Let \(t, s\) be self-isomorphisms of the trivial line bundle \(\mathcal O_V\) with
  \(t.\mathrm{hom} \mathbin{;} s.\mathrm{hom} = \mathbf 1\). Then pairing them through the
  iso-transport bifunctor and contracting via the unit comparison is trivial:
  \[
    \mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,t\,s \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}
      = \mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}.
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:tensorhom_inv_comp_leftunitor, def:tensorobjisoofiso, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso}
  \leanok
  Both \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,t\,s\) and \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}\) are sheafifications
  of presheaf-level constructions, so the claim is the image under the sheafification functor of a
  presheaf-level coherence. At the presheaf level the pairing is the monoidal tensor
  \((\,\mathrm{forget}\,t \otimes_{\mathrm m} \mathrm{forget}\,s\,)\) of the two underlying unit
  endomorphisms, whose hom-composite is the identity, and the contraction is the left unitor at the
  unit; \cref{lem:tensorhom_inv_comp_leftunitor} collapses the composite to the bare unitor.
  Functoriality of sheafification transports this to the stated sheaf-level identity.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}



[Naturality of the presheaf dual-of-unit iso against a unit automorphism (B1 eval-core)]
  \label{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}
  % NOTE: iter-099 — the v4.31 recovery dropped this decl from DualInverse.lean; it is currently a
  % private re-port in TensorObjInverse.lean. \lean{} name still resolves; pending upstream-restore decision.
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafDualUnitIso_naturality}
  \uses{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso, def:dual_unit_iso_gen, lem:internal_hom_eval, lem:presheaf_dualisoofiso_trans}
  Let \(\hat s\) be a presheaf-level automorphism of the unit \(\mathbf 1\) (the underlying map of a
  sheaf-level unit automorphism \(s\)). The presheaf dual-of-unit isomorphism
  \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso} : \mathtt{dual}\,\mathbf 1 \cong \mathbf 1\) is natural in
  \(\hat s\) for the contravariant transport on the source:
  \[
    \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\hat s \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}
      = \mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso} \;\mathbin{;}\; \hat s.
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso, def:dual_unit_iso_gen, lem:internal_hom_eval, lem:presheaf_dualisoofiso_trans}
  By construction (\cref{def:dual_unit_iso_gen}, \cref{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso})
  \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}\) is the sectionwise evaluation-at-\(1\) map of the internal hom of
  the unit into itself. On sections, \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\hat s\) is precomposition by the
  underlying map of \(\hat s\) (\cref{lem:presheaf_dualisoofiso_trans}), so the left-hand composite
  evaluates a dual section \(\varphi\) at \(1\) after precomposing by \(\hat s\), i.e.\ it computes
  \(\varphi(\hat s(1))\). Since \(\hat s\) is a unit automorphism --- multiplication by a unit scalar
  --- it commutes with evaluation, so this equals \(\hat s\) applied to \(\varphi(1)\), the
  right-hand composite. Equality of the two evaluation-at-\(1\) maps is the naturality of the
  internal-hom evaluation (\cref{lem:internal_hom_eval}) specialised at the unit.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}




\leanok
[Dual of a unit automorphism is itself (cocycle residual A, step 2a / B1)]
  \label{lem:dualunitiso_dualisoofiso}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.dualUnitIso_dualIsoOfIso}
  \uses{lem:dual_unit_iso, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso, lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality, def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso}
  Let \(s\) be a self-isomorphism of the trivial line bundle \(\mathcal O_V\). Conjugating its
  contravariant dual-transport by the unit-self-duality iso recovers \(s\):
  \[
    \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}^{-1}
      \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,s
      \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}
    \;=\; s .
  \]
  Equivalently, the sheaf-level dual of a unit automorphism, read through
  \(\mathtt{dual}\,\mathcal O_V \cong \mathcal O_V\), is the automorphism itself.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:dual_unit_iso, def:scheme_modules_dual_iso_of_iso, lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality, def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso}
  \leanok
  By pure iso-algebra the claim is equivalent to the single naturality square
  \[
    (\mathrm N):\qquad
    \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,s \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}
      = \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso} \;\mathbin{;}\; s,
  \]
  the naturality of \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso} : \mathtt{dual}\,\mathcal O_V \cong \mathcal O_V\) with
  respect to the unit automorphism \(s\), acting contravariantly through \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,s\)
  on the source. This is the genuine open obligation. It decomposes through the two layers of the
  construction \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso} = \mathtt{sheafify}(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso})
  \mathbin{;} \mathtt{counit}\):
  \begin{itemize}
    \item the presheaf eval-core naturality
      \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\hat s \mathbin{;} \mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}
        = \mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso} \mathbin{;} \hat s\)
      (\cref{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}, with \(\hat s\) the underlying presheaf map of
      \(s\)), pushed through the sheafification functor; and
    \item the sheafification-counit naturality
      \(\mathtt{sheafify}(\hat s) \mathbin{;} \mathtt{counit} = \mathtt{counit} \mathbin{;} s\),
      the naturality of the sheafification counit at the unit automorphism.
  \end{itemize}
  Composing the two squares gives \((\mathrm N)\), and the iso-algebra reduction then yields the
  stated identity.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}





\leanok
[Unit self-duality evaluation collapse (cocycle residual A, step 2)]
  \label{lem:tensorobj_unit_self_duality_collapse}
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_unit_self_duality_collapse}
  \uses{lem:tensorobjisoofiso_trans, lem:tensorobjisoofiso_refl, lem:dualisoofiso_trans, lem:dualisoofiso_refl, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:dual_unit_iso, lem:dualunitiso_dualisoofiso, lem:tensorobjisoofiso_comp_unit_iso}
  Let \(t\) be a self-isomorphism of the trivial line bundle \(\mathcal{O}_V\) on a
  trivialising open \(V\). Pairing \(t\) on the \(M\)-leg with the dual-transported transition
  on the \(N\)-leg --- which lands at the node \(\mathtt{dual}\,\mathcal{O}_V\) and so must be
  conjugated back to \(\mathcal{O}_V\) through the unit-self-duality iso
  \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso} : \mathtt{dual}\,\mathcal{O}_V \cong \mathcal{O}_V\)
  (\cref{lem:dual_unit_iso}) --- and then contracting via the unit comparison cancels:
  \[
    \mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\,t\,
      \bigl(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}^{-1}
            \;\mathbin{;}\;(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,t)^{-1}
            \;\mathbin{;}\;\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}\bigr)
    \;\mathbin{;}\;
    \mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}
    \;=\;
    \mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}.
  \]
  This is the \(g_{ij}\) (inside \(e^M_x\)) meeting \(g_{ij}^{-1} = \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,
  g_{ij}\) (inside \(e^N_x\)) cancellation: under the canonical evaluation of the free
  rank-one module against its dual, the transition iso and its inverse-transpose annihilate,
  leaving the trivialisation-independent unit contraction. The \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}\)
  conjugation on the \(N\)-leg is what makes both factors live on \(\mathcal{O}_V\) so the
  bifunctor \(\mathtt{tensorObjIsoOfIso}\) can pair them; under the eval-at-\(1\) semantics of
  \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}\) the conjugated dual leg reduces to \(t^{-1}\), so the pair is
  the symmetric \(t \otimes t^{-1}\) that the multiplication kills.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{lem:tensorobjisoofiso_trans, lem:tensorobjisoofiso_refl, lem:dualisoofiso_trans, lem:dualisoofiso_refl, lem:tensorobj_unit_iso, lem:dual_unit_iso, lem:dualunitiso_dualisoofiso, lem:tensorobjisoofiso_comp_unit_iso}
  \leanok
  The collapse factors through the two cocycle helpers rather than a direct sectionwise computation.
  First, the \(N\)-leg --- the dual-conjugated transition
  \(\mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}^{-1} \mathbin{;} (\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,t)^{-1} \mathbin{;}
  \mathtt{dual\_unit\_iso}\) --- is exactly \(t^{-1}\): inverting the dual-of-a-unit identity
  \cref{lem:dualunitiso_dualisoofiso} (applied to \(t\), read off its inverse) turns the conjugated
  transition into \(t^{-1}\). The collapse thus reduces to pairing \(t\) with \(t^{-1}\) on the two
  legs. Second, since \(t.\mathrm{hom} \mathbin{;} (t^{-1}).\mathrm{hom} = \mathbf 1\), the pairing
  contracts against the unit comparison by \cref{lem:tensorobjisoofiso_comp_unit_iso}, leaving
  \(\mathtt{tensorObj\_unit\_iso}\) alone. This is the \(g_{ij}\)-meets-\(g_{ij}^{-1}\) cancellation,
  now realised as the algebraic identity ``the dual of a unit-automorphism is itself, and a
  unit-automorphism paired with its inverse contracts to the unit''.
\end{proof}

\begin{remark}
[Alternative route via object-level descent (fallback)]
  \label{rem:dual_via_stack}
  Should the slice-and-sheafify route of
  \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom,lem:internal_hom_isSheaf} bottom out, the dual can
  instead be assembled by \emph{object-level descent}: glue the local trivial duals
  \(\mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) along the inverse-transpose transition isomorphisms
  \(g_{ij}^{-\mathsf{T}}\) of \(L\) into a global \(L^{-1}\) on \(X\). Abstractly this
  is effective descent for sheaves of modules on the small Zariski site --- the
  essential-surjectivity of the descent-data functor of an \(\mathtt{IsStack}\)
  pseudofunctor \(U \mapsto (\mathcal{O}_U\text{-modules})\). This is the more
  principled and reusable construction but the larger build (it requires constructing
  the restriction pseudofunctor and proving the stack/effective-descent property),
  and is recorded only as a fallback; the slice internal-hom route above is the
  intended one. It is the same morphism-/object-level descent family on which the
  associator re-route of \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso} also waits.
\end{remark}

\subsection{Internal-hom and restrict-scalars helper declarations}
\label{subsec:tensorobj_internalhom_helpers}

The following are the internal Lean helper declarations assembling the morphism
module \cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom_value}, the slice internal hom
\cref{def:presheaf_internal_hom}, its restriction maps
\cref{lem:presheaf_internal_hom_restriction}, the presheaf dual
\cref{def:presheaf_dual}, the evaluation morphism \cref{lem:internal_hom_eval}, the
lax-monoidal restrict-scalars structure \cref{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal}, the
ring-iso base-change equivalence \cref{lem:restrictscalars_ringiso_tensorequiv} and
the pushforward adjunction \texttt{presheaf\_pushforward\_adj\_substrate}. Each is
proved directly in Lean and carries the dependency edges of the construction.

\begin{definition}\leanok
[Canonical ring map from a terminal object]
  \label{def:term_ring_map}
  \lean{PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.termRingMap}
  \uses{def:presheaf_internal_hom_value}
  For a presheaf of commutative rings \(R\) on a base category with terminal object
  \(T\) and an object \(Y\), the unique morphism \(Y \to T\) induces the ring map
  \(R(T) \to R(Y)\) along which a global scalar acts on the value at \(Y\).
\end{definition}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:presheaf_internal_hom_value}
  \leanok
  Proved directly in Lean, as \(R\) applied to the opposite of the terminal morphism.
\end{proof}

\begin{lemma}\leanok
[Naturality of the terminal ring map]
  \label{lem:term_ring_map_naturality}
  \lean{PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.termRingMap_naturality}
  \uses{def:term_ring_map}
  The restriction map \(R(g) : R(X) \to R(Y)\) along \(g : X \to Y\) carries
  \(\mathtt{termRingMap}_X\,f\) to \(\mathtt{termRingMap}_Y\,f\), so the images of a
  global scalar \(f \in R(T)\) are compatible with restriction.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:term_ring_map}
  \leanok
  Proved directly in Lean, from uniqueness of morphisms into the terminal object and
  functoriality of \(R\).
\end{proof}

\begin{definition}\leanok
[Multiplication by a global scalar as an endomorphism]
  \label{def:global_smul}
  \lean{PresheafOfModules.InternalHom.globalSMul}
  \uses{def:term_ring_map, lem:term_ring_map_naturality}
  For \(f \in R(T)\), the endomorphism \(m_f^{N} : N \to N\) of a presheaf of modules
  \(N\) that at each object \(Y\) is multiplication by \(\mathtt{termRingMap}_Y\,f \in
  R(Y)\); its naturality is the heart of the global-scalar action on morphism modules.
\end{definition}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:term_ring_map, lem:term_ring_map_naturality}
  \leanok
  Proved directly in Lean: the naturality square commutes by
  \(\mathtt{termRingMap\_naturality}\) and the semilinearity of the restriction maps of
  \(N\).
\end{proof}

\begin{definition}\leanok
[Evaluation functional of a dual section]
  \label{def:eval_lin}
  \lean{PresheafOfModules.evalLin}

  At an object \(X\), the \(R(X)\)-linear map \(M(X) \to R(X)\) obtained by evaluating
  a dual section \(\varphi\) at its terminal component.
\end{definition}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:presheaf_dual}
